BAPTIST HEALTH®
BAPTIST HEALTH MEDICAL GROUP
PATIENT CONDITIONS AND CONSENTS
1. CONSENT TO DIAGNOSTIC TESTS, PROCEDURES, & MEDICAL TREATMENT:
| voluntarily consent to care involving routine diagnostic tests, procedures and medical treatment as ordered by my treating physician(s), including
their assistants or designees. In the case of diagnostic studies, | consent to the interpretation of the studies at an off-site location through the use of
telehealth. | consent to medical, nursing, allied health and other students observing and participating in my care under the supervision of a qualified
professional. | consent to photographic recordings or reproducible images during the surgical, medical, and/or diagnostic procedure(s) and their
use for scientific, educational, identification, or research purposes. | also consent to testing for communicable and blood-borne infectious diseases,
such as hepatitis, tuberculosis, and the human immunodeficiency virus (HIV), if a provider orders testing for diagnostic purposes or if there has
been an exposure to healthcare personnel. | have been given no guarantees about the results that may be obtained from my care.
2. PHYSICIANS AND OTHER HEALTH CARE PROVIDERS WHO ARE NOT EMPLOYEES OF BAPTIST HEALTH MEDICAL GROUP:
In most situations, the medical treatment provided to patients by Baptist Health Medical Group (“Baptist”) is provided by Baptist employed physicians
and providers. However, there other providers who are not employees or agents of Baptist that may provide medical services or be involved in my
care. Such providers include, but are not limited to radiologists, pathologists, and psychologists. These providers may be independent or may be
employed by other health care organizations. You may receive a separate bill for the services of these providers or the bill you receive may have
separate charges for services of such providers. Charges for such services are established by these providers.
3. CONSENT TO USE AND DISCLOSURE OF INFORMATION:
| authorize Baptist to use and disclose information about me to carry out treatment, payment and healthcare operations. Examples of such uses and
disclosures include, but are not limited to, providing information to: (a) any person, company, or entity (such as HMOs, insurance companies,
workers’ compensation carriers, my employer, the Medicare and Medicaid programs and their intermediaries and review organizations, and any
other payor or its review organization or third party administrator) that is or may be liable for paying any claim for benefits arising out of services
provided to me by Baptist; (b) any health care provider or ambulance company providing services for me; (c) any providers who may be providing
ongoing care to me such as hospitals, home health agencies, nursing homes, physicians, and other health care facilities; (d) any licensing or
accrediting organizations necessary for Baptist to obtain or maintain licensure or accreditation; (e) to providers or entities for the purposes of
conducting medical audits, utilization reviews, or quality assurance reviews; and (f) any other persons or entities described in Baptist’s Notice of
Privacy Practices. This consent includes the release of medical records and billing information related to drug-related conditions, alcoholism,
psychological and psychiatric conditions, and/or communicable or blood-borne infectious diseases, such as hepatitis, tuberculosis, and the human
immunodeficiency virus (HIV). | also consent to any third party payor or its review organization paying for my care to discuss my plan of treatment for
utilization review purposes. | also authorize the release of my social security number to the manufacturer of any implanted medical device in
accordance with the Medical Device Tracking Act.
4. FINANCIAL AGREEMENT AND ASSIGNMENT OF BENEFITS:
| agree that | am responsible for payment of my physician office bill. Payment of any portion of my bill not covered by a third party payor is due at the
time of service unless Baptist has agreed to other arrangements.
| agree to the assignment of all third party payor benefits to Baptist and to any health care provider rendering services to me. | agree to pay Baptist
and other health care providers for all charges for services that are not covered or paid by any third party payor regardless of the reason, including but
not limited to a determination by any third party payor that such services are not covered services or medically necessary. To the extent | ama
Medicare or Medicaid beneficiary, |certify that any information | provide in applying for payment under Title XVIII (Medicare) or Title XIX (Medicaid) of the
Social Security Act is correct. | request payment of authorized benefits to be made on my behalf to Baptist or to any health care provider rendering
services to me by the Medicare or Medicaid program. | hereby irrevocably appoint Baptist as my authorized representative to pursue any claims,
penalties, and administrative and/or legal remedies on my behalf for collection against any responsible payer, employer-sponsored medical benefit
plans, third party liability carrier or, any other responsible third party for any and all benefits due me for the payment of charges associated with my
treatment. This assignment shall not be construed as an obligation of Baptist to pursue any such right of recovery. | agree to take all actions
necessary to assist Baptist in collecting payment from any such third party payer.
After reasonable notice, any unpaid account may be turned over to a collection agency and/or attorney for collection. Should it be necessary for
Baptist to pursue collection, | agree to pay all reasonable collection costs, including court costs and attorney’s fees incurred by Baptist in collecting
my account.

5. SMOKE-FREE ENVIRONMENT:
Baptist maintains a smoke-free environment at each of its locations. Smoking is prohibited by health care personnel, patients, and visitors.
6. NOTICE OF NONDISCRIMINATION:
Baptist programs and activities are available regardless of race, color, national origin, religion, sex, disability or any other status protected by federal,
state or local law.

10. CONSENT TO WIRELESS TELEPHONE CALLS:
| hereby authorize Baptist and all third parties, including clinical providers who have provided care to me, along with any billing services, collection
agencies, attorneys or other agents who may work on their behalf (including their successors, assigns, affiliates, or agents), to contact me at any
telephone number associated with my account(s), including wireless telephone numbers or other numbers that may result in charges to me. |
agree that methods of contact may include using automatic telephone dialing systems or other computer assisted technology.
11. CONSENT TO EMAILOR TEXT USAGE FOR HEALTHCARE COMMUNICATIONS:
If at any time | provide an email address at which | may be contacted, | consent to receiving healthcare communication at that email from Baptist. |
further consent to Baptist communicating healthcare information, such as appointment reminders, to me on my wireless telephone through text.

Dear Patient,
In order torelease written prescriptions, including controlled substances; to someone other than you itis necessary

prescriptions are picked up.
Please note written prescriptions will not be given to anyone youhave not listed as an authorized pick up. If at any
time you wouldlike tomake changes toyour approved list, youmay do so bycompleting a new authorization form.

[DOB-78] - NM cor bone scan limited in BAPTIST HEALTH CORBIN NUCLEAR MEDICINE (continued)
Imaging (continued)
CLINICAL INDICATION: CHRONIC PAIN IN LEFT SHOULDER; M25.511-Pain in
right shoulder; G89.29-Other chronic pain
TECHNIQUE: 17.2 mCi of Tc-99m HDP were injected IV. Several hours later, small field-of-view imaging centered on the chest and shoulders was
performed.
COMPARISON: NONE 
FINDINGS: Low level increased tracer uptake of both shoulders in a
relatively symmetric pattern most consistent with arthritic type change. Low level increased tracer uptake of the sternoclavicular joint regions
and of the thoracic spine also most consistent with degenerative type
uptake. No intense areas of tracer activity noted in the humerus shaft
corresponding to abnormality on MRI. Both kidneys are visualized.
Impression:
1. No focal areas of increased tracer uptake involving the mid left
humeral diaphysis corresponding to abnormality seen on previous MRI. 2. Degenerative type uptake noted of the axial and appendicular
skeleton.

MRI HUMERUS LEFT WO CONTRASTREASON FOR EXAM: M25.512; M54,2-Cervicalgia; M25.512-Pain in left
shoulder
COMPARISON: Plain films of the humerus dated [DOB-3062]
Scans were obtained through the left humerus inthe sagittal, axial and
coronal planes using T1 and T2-weighted scans.
MRI Findings: In the mid shaft of the humerus there is some increased
signal focally inthe cortical bone of the hurnerus. This increased
signal is on both the T1 and T2 scans. The marrow signal was
unremarkable. There is no edema inthe area of the periosteum. Muscles
inthe arm were unremarkable. No other areas of abnormal signal are
noted inthe humerus.
IMPRESSION:
Focal abnormal signal inthe cortical bone of the mid shaft
of humerus on both the Ti and T2 sequences. This could represent
hemangioma. Would recommend consideration of bone scan especially in the
setting of pain in this region for further evaluation.
This report was finalized on 5/27/2022 8:06 AM by Dr. William Daniel II,

PREVENTING SURGICAL SITE INFECTIONS
· Call 911 or have someone take you to the Emergency Department if you have any of the following: · Sudden numbness or weakness of your face, arm or leg especially on one side of the body
· Sudden confusion, difficulty speaking or trouble understanding
· Changes in your vision or loss of sight in one eye
· Sudden severe headache with no known cause
· Sudden dizziness, trouble walking, loss of balance or coordination

[DOB-274] - NM cor injection 30 in BAPTIST HEALTH CORBIN NUCLEAR MEDICINE (continued)
Imaging (continued)
right shoulder; G89.29-Other chronic pain
TECHNIQUE: 17.2 mCi of Tc-99m HDP were injected IV. Several hours later, small field-of-view imaging centered on the chest and shoulders was
performed.
COMPARISON: NONE 
FINDINGS: Low level increased tracer uptake of both shoulders in a
relatively symmetric pattern most consistent with arthritic type change. Low level increased tracer uptake of the sternoclavicular joint regions
and of the thoracic spine also most consistent with degenerative type
uptake. No intense areas of tracer activity noted in the humerus shaft
corresponding to abnormality on MRI. Both kidneys are visualized.
Impression:
1. No focal areas of increased tracer uptake involving the mid left
humeral diaphysis corresponding to abnormality seen on previous MRI. 2. Degenerative type uptake noted of the axial and appendicular
skeleton.
This report was finalized on 7/5/2022 2:37 PM by Dr. Kevin J Croce, MD. Testing Performed By

2. PHYSICIANS AND OTHER HEALTH CARE PROVIDERS WHO ARE NOT EMPLOYEES OF THE HOSPITAL: Physicians and certified
registered nurse anesthetists rendering medical treatment to patients in the hospital are not employed by the Hospital. In most
situations, advanced practitioners, including but not limited to, nurse practitioners, physician assistants, surgical assistants and
psychologists, providing treatment to patients inthe Hospital are not employed by the Hospital. You may receive a separate bill for the
services of these providers, or the Hospital bill you receive may have separate charges for services of such providers. Charges for such
services are established by the providers.

accept payment from third party payors with whom itdoes not have a written signed agreement and that such acceptance of payment

Hospital and other health care providers for all charges for services that are not covered or paid by any third party payor regardless of the

· M25.511,G89.29 (ICD-10-CM) - Chronic right shoulder pain
PREVENTING SURGICAL SITE INFECTIONS
· Call 911 or have someone take you to the Emergency Department if you have any of the following: · Sudden numbness or weakness of your face, arm or leg especially on one side of the body
· Sudden confusion, difficulty speaking or trouble understanding
· Changes in your vision or loss of sight in one eye
· Sudden severe headache with no known cause
· Sudden dizziness, trouble walking, loss of balance or coordination

emergency help quickly, the powerful clot-dissolving medicines can reduce the disabilities caused by a

Why is smoking bad for me?
Smoking increases the risk of heart disease, lung disease, vascular disease, stroke, and cancer. If you

Quit Now Kentucky

Instructions for After Discharge
Patient Instructions
None · Call 911 or have someone take you to the Emergency Department if you have any of the following: · Sudden numbness or weakness of your face, arm or leg especially on one side of the body
· Sudden confusion, difficulty speaking or trouble understanding
· Changes in your vision or loss of sight in one eye
· Sudden severe headache with no known cause
· Sudden dizziness, trouble walking, loss of balance or coordination
It is important to seek emergency care right away if you have further stroke symptoms. If you get
emergency help quickly, the powerful clot-dissolving medicines can reduce the disabilities caused by a
stroke. 
For more information:
American Stroke Association
1-888-4-STROKE
www.strokeassociation.org
IF YOU SMOKE OR USE TOBACCO PLEASE READ THE FOLLOWING:

Why is smoking bad for me?
Smoking increases the risk of heart disease, lung disease, vascular disease, stroke, and cancer. If you
smoke, STOP!
For more information:
Quit Now Kentucky
1-800-QUIT-NOW
https://kentucky.quitlogix.org/en-US/
If you feel like life is too tough and are thinking of suicide or injuring yourself, get help right away! · Call or text 988 to speak to someone. Discharge

Visit Type: Office Visit
This 38 year old patient presents for MA Notes, Neck pain and Left Shoulder pain.
History of Present Illness
1. MA Notes
Pt is being seen because her chart needs to be upto date for her neuro surgeon andto
schedule an MRI.
2. Neck pain
The severity of the problem is moderate. The problem has worsened. The frequency of pain
is daily. Location of pain is bilateral posterior neck. The patient describes the pain as Aching
andThrobbing. The event(s) surrounding the occurrence of the symptom do not include
injury. Aggravating factors include flexion and turning head. Associated symptoms include
joint pain, muscle spasm and tenderness.
3. Left Shoulder pain
It occurs occasionally and is fluctuating. Location: left shoulder. The pain is aching and
throbbing. Context: there is no injury. The pain is aggravated by movement. Associated
symptoms include decreased mobility. Hand Dominance: left.

[DOB-2716] - Clinical Documentation Only in BAPTIST HEALTH CORBIN SOUTH IMAGING MRI (continued) · Call 911 or have someone take you to the Emergency Department if you have any of the following: · Sudden numbness or weakness of your face, arm or leg especially on one side of the body
· Sudden confusion, difficulty speaking or trouble understanding
· Changes in your vision or loss of sight in one eye
· Sudden severe headache with no known cause
· Sudden dizziness, trouble walking, loss of balance or coordination
It is important to seek emergency care right away if you have further stroke symptoms. If you get
emergency help quickly, the powerful clot-dissolving medicines can reduce the disabilities caused by a
stroke. 
For more information:
American Stroke Association
1-888-4-STROKE
www.strokeassociation.org
IF YOU SMOKE OR USE TOBACCO PLEASE READ THE FOLLOWING:
Why is smoking bad for me?
Smoking increases the risk of heart disease, lung disease, vascular disease, stroke, and cancer. If you
smoke, STOP!
For more information:
Quit Now Kentucky
1-800-QUIT-NOW
https://kentucky.quitlogix.org/en-US/
If you feel like life is too tough and are thinking of suicide or injuring yourself, get help right away! · Call or text 988 to speak to someone.

MRI Humerus Left Without Contrast [470911402] Resulted: 05/27/22 0806, Result status: Final result
Ordering provider: Gross, Theresa, APRN 05/26/22 1323 Order status: Completed
Resulted by: Daniel, William T II, MD Filed by: Interface, Rad Results Enterprise In 05/27/22 0809
Performed: 05/26/22 1328 - 05/26/22 1427 Accession number: [PHONE-2644]
Resulting lab: BAPTIST HEALTH RADIOLOGY
Narrative:
MRI HUMERUS LEFT WO CONTRAST- REASON FOR EXAM: M25.512; M54.2-Cervicalgia; M25.512-Pain in left
shoulder
COMPARISON: Plain films of the humerus dated [DOB-2643]
Scans were obtained through the left humerus in the sagittal, axial and
coronal planes using T1 and T2-weighted scans. MRI Findings: In the mid shaft of the humerus there is some increased
signal focally in the cortical bone of the humerus. This increased
signal is on both the T1 and T2 scans. The marrow signal was
unremarkable. There is no edema in the area of the periosteum. Muscles
in the arm were unremarkable. No other areas of abnormal signal are
noted in the humerus.
Impression:
Focal abnormal signal in the cortical bone of the mid shaft
of humerus on both the T1 and T2 sequences. This could represent
hemangioma. Would recommend consideration of bone scan especially in the
setting of pain in this region for further evaluation. This report was finalized on 5/27/2022 8:06 AM by Dr. William Daniel II, MD.

1. CONSENT TO DIAGNOSTIC TESTS, PROCEDURES, & MEDICALTREATMENT:| voluntarily consent to (and consent on behalf of any
child born to me while hospitalized) care involving routine diagnostic tests, procedures and medical treatment as ordered by my
admitting and treating physicians, including their assistants or designees. | consent to receive medical care through the use of
telehealth and/or remote patient monitoring. |further consent to the interpretation of diagnostic studies from an off-site location using
telehealth technologies. | consent to medical, nursing, allied health and other students observing and participating in my care under the
supervision of a qualified professional. | consent to photographic recordings or reproducible images during the surgical, medical, and/or
diagnostic procedure(s) and their use for identification, scientific, educational, or research purposes. |also consent to testing for
communicable and blood-borne infectious diseases, such as hepatitis, tuberculosis, and the human immunodeficiency virus (HIV), if a
provider orders testing for diagnostic purposes or if there has been an exposure to healthcare personnel. | have been given no
guarantees about the results that may be obtained from my care. | understand and agree that the results of diagnostic tests, procedures
and medical treatment ordered and provided by providers while | am hospitalized, including a notice of admission to the hospital, a
notice of discharge from the hospital, and/or notice of transfer from the hospital to another facility will be provided to my primary care
physician, physician group and/or post acute care provider on record, if any.
2. PHYSICIANS AND OTHER HEALTH CARE PROVIDERS WHO ARE NOT EMPLOYEES OF THE HOSPITAL: Physicians and certified
registered nurse anesthetists rendering medical treatment to patients in the hospital are not employed by the Hospital. In most
situations, advanced practitioners, including but not limited to, nurse practitioners, physician assistants, surgical assistants and
psychologists, providing treatment to patients inthe Hospital are not employed by the Hospital. You may receive a separate bill for the
services of these providers, or the Hospital bill you receive may have separate charges for services of such providers. Charges for such
services are established by the providers.
3. FINANCIAL AGREEMENT AND ASSIGNMENT OF BENEFITS:| agree that | am responsible for payment of my Hospital bill. Payment
of any portion of my bill not covered by a third party payor is due upon discharge from the Hospital unless the Hospital has agreed to
other arrangements. | agree to the assignment of all third party payor benefits to the Hospital and to any health care provider rendering
services to me. | acknowledge and agree that Hospital is not required to accept assignment of any third party payor benefits, in which
case, | may receive a bill from Hospital for the full amount of charges related to any care or treatment provided to me or my guarantee by
Hospital and | agree to pay the Hospital and other health care providers for such charges. Moreover, |understand that Hospital may
accept payment from third party payors with whom itdoes not have a written signed agreement and that such acceptance of payment
does not constitute acceptance by Hospital of any reimbursement rates established by such third party payors and that | may receive a
bill from Hospital for the difference between the rate paid by such third party payors and the Hospital’s charges. | agree to pay the
Hospital and other health care providers for all charges for services that are not covered or paid by any third party payor regardless of the
reason, including but not limited to a determination by any third party payor that such services are not covered services or medically
necessary. To the extent |am a Medicare or Medicaid beneficiary, | certify that any information | provide inapplying for payment under
Title XVIII (Medicare) or Title XIX (Medicaid) of the Social Security Act is correct. | request payment of authorized benefits to be made on
my behalf to Baptist Health facility or to any health care provider rendering services to me by the Medicare or Medicaid program. |
hereby irrevocably appoint Hospital as my authorized representative to pursue any claims, penalties, and administrative and/or legal
remedies on my behalf for collection against any responsible payer, employer-sponsored medical benefit plans, third party liability
carrier or, any other responsible third party for any and all benefits due me for the payment of charges associated with my treatment.
This assignment shall not be construed as an obligation of the Hospital to pursue any such right of recovery. | agree to take all actions
necessary to assist Hospital in collecting payment from any such third party payer. | understand that after reasonable notice, any
unpaid account may be turned over to a collection agency and/or attorney for collection. Should it be necessary for the Hospital to
pursue collection, | agree to pay all reasonable collection costs, including court costs and attorney’s fees incurred by the Hospital in
collecting my account. Pursuant to the Fair Credit Reporting Act (15 USC §1681b(a)(2)), | authorize a credit reporting agency engaged
by Hospital or its collection agents to release to the Hospital or any of its representatives or affiliates, my consumer report. |
understand that the purpose of this authorization and request is to obtain my consumer report, which may be used to determine the
availability of or the need for financial assistance, charity care, or insurance coverage for me and may be used for billing and collection
purposes related to payment for services provided to me. | understand this authorization is valid until my Hospital account for all
services is closed.

Instructions for After Discharge
Patient Instructions
None · Call 911 or have someone take you to the Emergency Department if you have any of the following: · Sudden numbness or weakness of your face, arm or leg especially on one side of the body
· Sudden confusion, difficulty speaking or trouble understanding
· Changes in your vision or loss of sight in one eye
· Sudden severe headache with no known cause
· Sudden dizziness, trouble walking, loss of balance or coordination
It is important to seek emergency care right away if you have further stroke symptoms. If you get
emergency help quickly, the powerful clot-dissolving medicines can reduce the disabilities caused by a
stroke. 
For more information:
American Stroke Association
1-888-4-STROKE
www.strokeassociation.org
IF YOU SMOKE OR USE TOBACCO PLEASE READ THE FOLLOWING:
Why is smoking bad for me?
Smoking increases the risk of heart disease, lung disease, vascular disease, stroke, and cancer. If you
smoke, STOP!

communicable and blood-borne infectious diseases, such as hepatitis, tuberculosis, and the human immunodeficiency virus (HIV), if a
provider orders testing for diagnostic purposes or if there has been an exposure to healthcare personnel. | have been given no
guarantees about the results that may be obtained from my care. | understand and agree that the results of diagnostic tests, procedures
and medical treatment ordered and provided by providers while | am hospitalized, including a notice of admission to the hospital, a
notice of discharge from the hospital, and/or notice of transfer from the hospital to another facility will be provided to my primary care
physician, physician group and/or post acute care provider on record, if any.
2. PHYSICIANS AND OTHER HEALTH CARE PROVIDERS WHO ARE NOT EMPLOYEES OF THE HOSPITAL: Physicians and certified
registered nurse anesthetists rendering medical treatment to patients in the hospital are not employed by the Hospital. In most
situations, advanced practitioners, including but not limited to, nurse practitioners, physician assistants, surgical assistants and
psychologists, providing treatment to patients inthe Hospital are not employed by the Hospital. You may receive a separate bill for the
services of these providers, or the Hospital bill you receive may have separate charges for services of such providers. Charges for such
services are established by the providers.
3. FINANCIAL AGREEMENT AND ASSIGNMENT OF BENEFITS:| agree that | am responsible for payment of my Hospital bill. Payment
of any portion of my bill not covered by a third party payor is due upon discharge from the Hospital unless the Hospital has agreed to
other arrangements. | agree to the assignment of all third party payor benefits to the Hospital and to any health care provider rendering
services to me. | acknowledge and agree that Hospital is not required to accept assignment of any third party payor benefits, in which
case, | may receive a bill from Hospital for the full amount of charges related to any care or treatment provided to me or my guarantee by
Hospital and | agree to pay the Hospital and other health care providers for such charges. Moreover, |understand that Hospital may
accept payment from third party payors with whom itdoes not have a written signed agreement and that such acceptance of payment
does not constitute acceptance by Hospital of any reimbursement rates established by such third party payors and that | may receive a
bill from Hospital for the difference between the rate paid by such third party payors and the Hospital’s charges. | agree to pay the
Hospital and other health care providers for all charges for services that are not covered or paid by any third party payor regardless of the
reason, including but not limited to a determination by any third party payor that such services are not covered services or medically
necessary. To the extent |am a Medicare or Medicaid beneficiary, | certify that any information | provide inapplying for payment under
Title XVIII (Medicare) or Title XIX (Medicaid) of the Social Security Act is correct. | request payment of authorized benefits to be made on
my behalf to Baptist Health facility or to any health care provider rendering services to me by the Medicare or Medicaid program. |
hereby irrevocably appoint Hospital as my authorized representative to pursue any claims, penalties, and administrative and/or legal
remedies on my behalf for collection against any responsible payer, employer-sponsored medical benefit plans, third party liability
carrier or, any other responsible third party for any and all benefits due me for the payment of charges associated with my treatment.
This assignment shall not be construed as an obligation of the Hospital to pursue any such right of recovery. | agree to take all actions
necessary to assist Hospital in collecting payment from any such third party payer. | understand that after reasonable notice, any
unpaid account may be turned over to a collection agency and/or attorney for collection. Should it be necessary for the Hospital to

collecting my account. Pursuant to the Fair Credit Reporting Act (15 USC §1681b(a)(2)), | authorize a credit reporting agency engaged

understand that the purpose of this authorization and request is to obtain my consumer report, which may be used to determine the

purposes related to payment for services provided to me. | understand this authorization is valid until my Hospital account for all

Patient Instructions (continued)
None · Call 911 or have someone take you to the Emergency Department if you have any of the following: · Sudden numbness or weakness of your face, arm or leg especially on one side of the body
· Sudden confusion, difficulty speaking or trouble understanding
· Changes in your vision or loss of sight in one eye
· Sudden severe headache with no known cause
· Sudden dizziness, trouble walking, loss of balance or coordination
It is important to seek emergency care right away if you have further stroke symptoms. If you get
emergency help quickly, the powerful clot-dissolving medicines can reduce the disabilities caused by a
stroke. 
For more information:
American Stroke Association
1-888-4-STROKE
www.strokeassociation.org
IF YOU SMOKE OR USE TOBACCO PLEASE READ THE FOLLOWING:
Why is smoking bad for me?
Smoking increases the risk of heart disease, lung disease, vascular disease, stroke, and cancer. If you
smoke, STOP!
For more information:
Quit Now Kentucky
1-800-QUIT-NOW
https://kentucky.quitlogix.org/en-US/
If you feel like life is too tough and are thinking of suicide or injuring yourself, get help right away! · Call or text 988 to speak to someone.

2. PHYSICIANS AND OTHER HEALTH CARE PROVIDERS WHO ARE NOT EMPLOYEES OF THE HOSPITAL: Physicians and certified
registered nurse anesthetists rendering medical treatment to patients in the hospital are not employed by the Hospital. In most

psychologists, providing treatment to patients inthe Hospital are not employed by the Hospital. You may receive a separate bill for the
services of these providers, or the Hospital bill you receive may have separate charges for services of such providers. Charges for such
services are established by the providers.
3. FINANCIAL AGREEMENT AND ASSIGNMENT OF BENEFITS:| agree that | am responsible for payment of my Hospital bill. Payment

services to me. | acknowledge and agree that Hospital is not required to accept assignment of any third party payor benefits, in which
case, | may receive a bill from Hospital for the full amount of charges related to any care or treatment provided to me or my guarantee by
Hospital and | agree to pay the Hospital and other health care providers for such charges. Moreover, |understand that Hospital may
accept payment from third party payors with whom itdoes not have a written signed agreement and that such acceptance of payment
does not constitute acceptance by Hospital of any reimbursement rates established by such third party payors and that | may receive a
bill from Hospital for the difference between the rate paid by such third party payors and the Hospital’s charges. | agree to pay the
Hospital and other health care providers for all charges for services that are not covered or paid by any third party payor regardless of the
reason, including but not limited to a determination by any third party payor that such services are not covered services or medically
necessary. To the extent |am a Medicare or Medicaid beneficiary, | certify that any information | provide inapplying for payment under
Title XVIII (Medicare) or Title XIX (Medicaid) of the Social Security Act is correct. | request payment of authorized benefits to be made on

Patient Instructions (continued)
None · Call 911 or have someone take you to the Emergency Department if you have any of the following: · Sudden numbness or weakness of your face, arm or leg especially on one side of the body
· Sudden confusion, difficulty speaking or trouble understanding
· Changes in your vision or loss of sight in one eye
· Sudden severe headache with no known cause
· Sudden dizziness, trouble walking, loss of balance or coordination
It is important to seek emergency care right away if you have further stroke symptoms. If you get
emergency help quickly, the powerful clot-dissolving medicines can reduce the disabilities caused by a
stroke. 
For more information:
American Stroke Association
1-888-4-STROKE
www.strokeassociation.org
IF YOU SMOKE OR USE TOBACCO PLEASE READ THE FOLLOWING:
Why is smoking bad for me?
Smoking increases the risk of heart disease, lung disease, vascular disease, stroke, and cancer. If you
smoke, STOP!
For more information:
Quit Now Kentucky
1-800-QUIT-NOW
https://kentucky.quitlogix.org/en-US/
If you feel like life is too tough and are thinking of suicide or injuring yourself, get help right away! · Call or text 988 to speak to someone.

Instructions for After Discharge
Patient Instructions
None · Call 911 or have someone take you to the Emergency Department if you have any of the following: · Sudden numbness or weakness of your face, arm or leg especially on one side of the body
· Sudden confusion, difficulty speaking or trouble understanding
· Changes in your vision or loss of sight in one eye
· Sudden severe headache with no known cause
· Sudden dizziness, trouble walking, loss of balance or coordination
It is important to seek emergency care right away if you have further stroke symptoms. If you get
emergency help quickly, the powerful clot-dissolving medicines can reduce the disabilities caused by a
stroke. 
For more information:
American Stroke Association
1-888-4-STROKE
www.strokeassociation.org
IF YOU SMOKE OR USE TOBACCO PLEASE READ THE FOLLOWING:
Why is smoking bad for me?
Smoking increases the risk of heart disease, lung disease, vascular disease, stroke, and cancer. If you
smoke, STOP!
For more information:
Quit Now Kentucky
1-800-QUIT-NOW
https://kentucky.quitlogix.org/en-US/

Impression Medications reviewed, Take medications aas prescribed. Side effects discussed and if
experience any side effect stop medication and return to clinic. Counsel on medication
compliance. Advised of proper body mechanics. No heavy lifting over 10 Ibs. No
repetitive bending at waist. Change positions frequently, may apply heat/ice to affected
area for 20 minutes for every 3-4 hours. Discussed benefits of maintaining healthy
weight and, if no improvement return one week, will discuss ordering physical therapy if
needed atnext visit. If develop any signs/symptoms ofcauda equina syndrome such as
loss of bowel loss of bladder; urinary retention; severe numbness/tingling in both legs
call|911/go.to Emergency room immediately. Patient verbalized understanding ¢of plan..

INPATIENT AND OUTPATIENT CONDITIONS AND CONSENTS CSN: 91106376531 ADMIT DATE:
1. CONSENT TO DIAGNOSTIC TESTS, PROCEDURES, & MEDICALTREATMENT:| voluntarily consent to (and consent on behalf of any
child born to me while hospitalized) care involving routine diagnostic tests, procedures and medical treatment as ordered by my
admitting and treating physicians, including their assistants or designees. | consent to receive medical care through the use of
telehealth and/or remote patient monitoring. |further consent to the interpretation of diagnostic studies from an off-site location using
telehealth technologies. | consent to medical, nursing, allied health and other students observing and participating in my care under the
supervision of a qualified professional. | consent to photographic recordings or reproducible images during the surgical, medical, and/or
diagnostic procedure(s) and their use for identification, scientific, educational, or research purposes. |also consent to testing for
communicable and blood-borne infectious diseases, such as hepatitis, tuberculosis, and the human immunodeficiency virus (HIV), if a
provider orders testing for diagnostic purposes or if there has been an exposure to healthcare personnel. | have been given no
guarantees about the results that may be obtained from my care. | understand and agree that the results of diagnostic tests, procedures
and medical treatment ordered and provided by providers while | am hospitalized, including a notice of admission to the hospital will be
provided to my primary care physician on record, if any.
2. PHYSICIANS AND OTHER HEALTH CARE PROVIDERS WHO ARE NOT EMPLOYEES OF THE HOSPITAL: Physicians and certified
registered nurse anesthetists rendering medical treatment to patients in the hospital are not employed by the Hospital. In most
situations, advanced practitioners, including but not limited to, nurse practitioners, physician assistants, surgical assistants and
psychologists, providing treatment to patients inthe Hospital are not employed by the Hospital. You may receive a separate bill for the
services of these providers, or the Hospital bill you receive may have separate charges for services of such providers. Charges for such
services are established by the providers.
3. FINANCIAL AGREEMENT AND ASSIGNMENT OF BENEFITS:| agree that | am responsible for payment of my Hospital bill. Payment
of any portion of my bill not covered by a third party payor is due upon discharge from the Hospital unless the Hospital has agreed to
other arrangements. | agree to the assignment of all third party payor benefits to the Hospital and to any health care provider rendering
services to me. | acknowledge and agree that Hospital is not required to accept assignment of any third party payor benefits, in which
case, | may receive a bill from Hospital for the full amount of charges related to any care or treatment provided to me or my guarantee by
Hospital and | agree to pay the Hospital and other health care providers for such charges. Moreover, |understand that Hospital may
accept payment from third party payors with whom itdoes not have a written signed agreement and that such acceptance of payment
does not constitute acceptance by Hospital of any reimbursement rates established by such third party payors and that | may receive a
bill from Hospital for the difference between the rate paid by such third party payors and the Hospital’s charges. | agree to pay the
Hospital and other health care providers for all charges for services that are not covered or paid by any third party payor regardless of the
reason, including but not limited to a determination by any third party payor that such services are not covered services or medically
necessary. To the extent |am a Medicare or Medicaid beneficiary, | certify that any information | provide inapplying for payment under
Title XVIII (Medicare) or Title XIX (Medicaid) of the Social Security Act is correct. | request payment of authorized benefits to be made on
my behalf to Baptist Health facility or to any health care provider rendering services to me by the Medicare or Medicaid program. |
hereby irrevocably appoint Hospital as my authorized representative to pursue any claims, penalties, and administrative and/or legal
remedies on my behalf for collection against any responsible payer, employer-sponsored medical benefit plans, third party liability
carrier or, any other responsible third party for any and all benefits due me for the payment of charges associated with my treatment.
This assignment shall not be construed as an obligation of the Hospital to pursue any such right of recovery. | agree to take all actions
necessary to assist Hospital in collecting payment from any such third party payer. | understand that after reasonable notice, any
unpaid account may be turned over to a collection agency and/or attorney for collection. Should it be necessary for the Hospital to
pursue collection, | agree to pay all reasonable collection costs, including court costs and attorney’s fees incurred by the Hospital in
collecting my account. Pursuant to the Fair Credit Reporting Act (15 USC §1681b(a)(2)), | authorize a credit reporting agency engaged
by Hospital or its collection agents to release to the Hospital or any of its representatives or affiliates, my consumer report. |
understand that the purpose of this authorization and request is to obtain my consumer report, which may be used to determine the
availability of or the need for financial assistance, charity care, or insurance coverage for me and may be used for billing and collection
purposes related to payment for services provided to me. | understand this authorization is valid until my Hospital account for all
services is closed.
4. OUTPATIENT MEDICARE PATIENTS: Medicare does not cover prescription drugs with only a few exceptions. | acknowledge that |am

Patient Instructions
None · Call 911 or have someone take you to the Emergency Department if you have any of the following: · Sudden numbness or weakness of your face, arm or leg especially on one side of the body
· Sudden confusion, difficulty speaking or trouble understanding
· Changes in your vision or loss of sight in one eye
· Sudden severe headache with no known cause
· Sudden dizziness, trouble walking, loss of balance or coordination
It is important to seek emergency care right away if you have further stroke symptoms. If you get
emergency help quickly, the powerful clot-dissolving medicines can reduce the disabilities caused by a
stroke.

[DOB-1247] - FL lou contrast injection ct mri in BAPTIST HEALTH CORBIN XRAY (continued)
Imaging (continued)
FL CONTRAST INJECTION CT/MRI- CLINICAL INDICATION: Left shoulder pain; M25.512-Pain in left shoulder
COMPARISON: None available. Total fluoroscopy time 2.08 minutes. FINDINGS: Preliminary images show no fracture or dislocation. Preservation of the joint space. The procedure and possible complications were discussed with the
patient. She stated that she understood and wished to continue. Fluoroscopy was used to localize a site for entry. Site was prepped and
draped in the usual sterile fashion. Topical anesthesia was provided
with lidocaine. A 22-gauge needle was then inserted into the left
glenohumeral joint. A mixture of bupivacaine, iodinated contrast, and
gadolinium as well as sterile saline was injected into the joint space. Some of the injection was extraarticular. No evidence of a tear of the supraspinatus tendon on the study. Patient was asked to exercise her shoulder and subsequently was taken to
MRI.
Impression:
Technically successful left shoulder arthrogram. No tear
seen on this portion of the exam. MRI is pending. This report was finalized on 6/24/2020 11:42 AM by Dr. Harold Reedy, MD. Testing Performed By

advisable during the course of the procedure.

If you wish to not receive blood or blood products please request to sign a refusal form. 1) Some surgical procedures may require
blood transfusions or blood products. | was informed that| may need to have blood products administered during the course of my
treatment. 2) It has been explained to me that the administration of blood products involves the introduction of the product into one
of myveins, commonly in the arm using a needle. | understand that the amount of blood products administered is determined by my
provider based upon myparticular needs. 3) It has been explained to me that the administration of blood products may expose me to
certain risks. Minor or temporary reactions, such as slight bruising, swelling, or local infection of the area where the needle pierces
the skin is not uncommon. Non-serious reactions may include headache, fever, or a mild skin reaction, such as itching or a rash.
Rare, but serious reactions are possible such as the transmission of diseases including hepatitis and AIDS, which require lengthy
treatment and can be fatal. It has been further explained that while procedures for screening blood products for diseases such as
AIDS and hepatitis are effective, these procedures are not perfect and there is a risk of exposure to AIDS, hepatitis, or other bloodborne disease. |have taken allof these risks into consideration and have decided that the benefits of receiving blood products
outweigh the risks. 4) |have been made aware of the reasonably available alternatives to the administration of blood products and
the risks and benefits of those alternatives including autologous blood transfusion, directed donation and not having any blood
products administered. | understand that | may be able to donate one of several units of my own blood to be used during planned
elective surgery and that my provider must determine my ability to donate for myself. This is called autologous transfusion. If1
choose to have an autologous transfusion, | understand that the last unit of blood must bedonated one week prior to expected use.
| also understand that | may choosea direct donation. A direct donation means | am allowed to select my own donors. There is no
scientific evidence that directed donations are safer but | understand many patients feel more comfortable designating their own
donors. The designated donor can donate within thirty (30) days of the anticipated need for the blood. It takes at least seventy-two
(72) hours for a unit from a directed donor to be processed and available for transfusion. These arrangements must be made by my
provider. Additionally, my procedure(s) may require the services of a perfusionist; a perfusionist assists in the management of the
lungs anc blood flow during heart surgery.

OPERATIVE / PROCEDURAL PERMIT
(Anesthesia & Related Procedures)
MEDIA REPRESENTATION
I consent to the taking of photographs, motion pictures, or videotapes during this procedure(s}. | authorize cameramen,
photographers, technicians, and equipment designed for such purposes to beadmitted to the operating room. | understand that
the photographs, motion pictures, or videotapes may become part of my medical record and may be used for scientific and/or
educational purposes as well as for mycontinued medical care. Provided, however, inthe event such is used for scientific and/or
educational purposes, my name will not be used and| will not beotherwise identified. | release Baptist Health, its agents,
servants, employees, consultants, and providers from any liability inconnection with the use of the photographs, pictures, or
videotapes.
TISSUE REMOVAL
Tunderstand that this procedure(s) may result inthe removal of bodytissue or body parts. | consent to their disposal according to
the hospital policies and the recommendations of the provider.
VENDOR REPRESENTATIVES / OBSERVERS
l understand thatthere may bea representative from a company thatfurnishes equipment to providers andhospitals present in
the room during mysurgical procedure. | also understand that his/her function is strictly to observe and advise, and that this
person has no part inmy direct care. | have been informed and understand that, when specifically requested by the provider, a
limited and specified number of individuals (excluding parents and blood relatives) are permitted to observe an operation or
procedure. | hereby consent to allow those individuals, specifically authorized by the provider, to observe the operation or
procedure consented to above.
CLINICAL STUDENTS
Baptist Health participates in clinical education programs with colleges and universities to give students, including but not limited
to, nursing students, medical students, interns, and residents (“students”), experience in clinical practice. Your provider may
permit students to observe and participate inyour care under the provider's direct supervision.
ADVANCE DIRECTIVE
acknowledge and agree that any advance directive including, but not limitedto, a do not resuscitate order, a medical order for
scope of treatment or an advance directive which outline my desire for a level of treatment other than full treatment will be
temporarily suspended during the procedure unless otherwise requested by me and documented by myphysician in the medical
record.
ANESTHESIA
understand that this procedure may require anesthesia. An anesthesiology provider has or will explain the potential benefits,
tisks, side effects, and potential anesthesia alternatives. If your provider recommends and will manage local anesthesia or
moderate sedation as noted below, this discussion will occur with your provider. Complications from any type of anesthetic are
infrequent, but do occur. Complications are more frequent for patients in poor health than for patients in good health.
Complications associated with alltypes of anesthesia include temporary or permanent damage to teeth, lips, nose, throat, voice
box, windpipe, eyes, nerves, or blood vessels. Drug reaction may occur and may be minor or serious even requiring
resuscitation. Breathing problems may develop. Headache, back pain, some degree of awareness, infection, nausea and/or
vomiting may occur. There are some very serious complications, which are uncommon, but can occur. These include paralysis,
brain damage, or death. There are other potential complications depending onyour situation, however, itis not possible to advise
youof every imaginable complication. Serious complications are unlikely. The benefits of surgery and anesthesia, except in
extreme circumstances, outweigh the potential risks. |agree thatthe potential benefit of the procedure(s) outweighs the potential
risks of anesthesia.
| understand that myprocedure is scheduled to be done with one or more of the following:
General Anesthesia Monitoring and care provided by the anesthesiologist, a Certified Registered Nurse
Anesthetist [CRNA], or anAnesthesiologist's Assistant [AA].
Monitored Anesthesia Care Possible sedation, monitoring and care provided by the anesthesiologist, a CRNA, or
an AA.
Regional Anesthesia Monitoring and care provided by the anesthesiologist, CRNA, or an AA.
Local Anesthesia Care managed by the Provider and monitored by a registered nurse.
Moderate Sedation Care managed by the Provider and monitored by a registered nurse.

| understand that MRI Scan may require the injection of contrast (a drug) into my bloodstream to assist in
visualization of certain areas in my body. A smal! number of patients may be allergic to contrast and occasionally
experience these reactions: headache, nausea, or coldness at the injection site. Very, very, rarely, other adverse
reactions are possible such as chest pain, rash, impaired breathing, or death. There is no means of testing you for
an allergic reaction before injecting the contrast. | understand that if complications occur, additional medica!
treatment may be required and emergency medica! treatment is available.

INPATIENT AND OUTPATIENT CONDITIONS AND CONSENTS CSN: 91082876279 ADMIT DATE:
1. CONSENT TO DIAGNOSTIC TESTS, PROCEDURES, & MEDICALTREATMENT:| voluntarily consent to (and consent on behalf of any
child born to me while hospitalized) care involving routine diagnostic tests, procedures and medical treatment as ordered by my
admitting and treating physicians, including their assistants or designees. | consent to receive medical care through the use of
telehealth and/or remote patient monitoring. |further consent to the interpretation of diagnostic studies from an off-site location using
telehealth technologies. | consent to medical, nursing, allied health and other students observing and participating in my care under the
supervision of a qualified professional. | consent to photographic recordings or reproducible images during the surgical, medical, and/or
diagnostic procedure(s) and their use for identification, scientific, educational, or research purposes. |also consent to testing for
communicable and blood-borne infectious diseases, such as hepatitis, tuberculosis, and the human immunodeficiency virus (HIV), if a
provider orders testing for diagnostic purposes or if there has been an exposure to healthcare personnel. | have been given no
guarantees about the results that may be obtained from my care.
2. PHYSICIANS AND OTHER HEALTH CARE PROVIDERS WHO ARE NOT EMPLOYEES OF THE HOSPITAL: Physicians and certified
registered nurse anesthetists rendering medical treatment to patients in the hospital are not employed by the Hospital. In most
situations, advanced practitioners, including but not limited to, nurse practitioners, physician assistants, surgical assistants and
psychologists, providing treatment to patients inthe Hospital are not employed by the Hospital. You may receive a separate bill for the
services of these providers, or the Hospital bill you receive may have separate charges for services of such providers. Charges for such
services are established by the providers.
3. FINANCIAL AGREEMENT AND ASSIGNMENT OF BENEFITS:| agree that | am responsible for payment of my Hospital bill. Payment
of any portion of my bill not covered by a third party payor is due upon discharge from the Hospital unless the Hospital has agreed to
other arrangements. | agree to the assignment of all third party payor benefits to the Hospital and to any health care provider rendering
services to me. | acknowledge and agree that Hospital is not required to accept assignment of any third party payor benefits, in which
case, | may receive a bill from Hospital for the full amount of charges related to any care or treatment provided to me or my guarantee by
Hospital and | agree to pay the Hospital and other health care providers for such charges. Moreover, |understand that Hospital may
accept payment from third party payors with whom itdoes not have a written signed agreement and that such acceptance of payment
does not constitute acceptance by Hospital of any reimbursement rates established by such third party payors and that | may receive a
bill from Hospital for the difference between the rate paid by such third party payors and the Hospital’s charges. | agree to pay the
Hospital and other health care providers for all charges for services that are not covered or paid by any third party payor regardless of the
reason, including but not limited to a determination by any third party payor that such services are not covered services or medically
necessary. To the extent |am a Medicare or Medicaid beneficiary, | certify that any information | provide inapplying for payment under
Title XVIII (Medicare) or Title XIX (Medicaid) of the Social Security Act is correct. | request payment of authorized benefits to be made on
my behalf to Baptist Health facility or to any health care provider rendering services to me by the Medicare or Medicaid program. |
hereby irrevocably appoint Hospital as my authorized representative to pursue any claims, penalties, and administrative and/or legal
remedies on my behalf for collection against any responsible payer, employer-sponsored medical benefit plans, third party liability
carrier or, any other responsible third party for any and all benefits due me for the payment of charges associated with my treatment.
This assignment shall not be construed as an obligation of the Hospital to pursue any such right of recovery. | agree to take all actions
necessary to assist Hospital in collecting payment from any such third party payer. | understand that after reasonable notice, any
unpaid account may be turned over to a collection agency and/or attorney for collection. Should it be necessary for the Hospital to
pursue collection, | agree to pay all reasonable collection costs, including court costs and attorney’s fees incurred by the Hospital in
collecting my account. Pursuant to the Fair Credit Reporting Act (15 USC §1681b(a)(2)), | authorize a credit reporting agency engaged
by Hospital or its collection agents to release to the Hospital or any of its representatives or affiliates, my consumer report. |
understand that the purpose of this authorization and request is to obtain my consumer report, which may be used to determine the
availability of or the need for financial assistance, charity care, or insurance coverage for me and may be used for billing and collection
purposes related to payment for services provided to me. | understand this authorization is valid until my Hospital account for all
services is closed.
4. OUTPATIENT MEDICARE PATIENTS: Medicare does not cover prescription drugs with only a few exceptions. | acknowledge that |am
responsible for any drugs furnished to me while an outpatient that meet Medicare’s definition of a prescription drug. These drugs are
also referred to as self-administered but they may be administered to me by Hospital personnel. Hospital may bill Medicare patients or

· M25.512 (ICD-10-CM) - Left shoulder pain
PREVENTING SURGICAL SITE INFECTIONS
· Call 911 or have someone take you to the Emergency Department if you have any of the following: · Sudden numbness or weakness of your face, arm or leg especially on one side of the body
· Sudden confusion, difficulty speaking or trouble understanding
· Changes in your vision or loss of sight in one eye
· Sudden severe headache with no known cause
· Sudden dizziness, trouble walking, loss of balance or coordination
It is important to seek emergency care right away if you have further stroke symptoms. If you get

MRI Shoulder Left Arthrogram [272862387] Resulted: 06/24/20 1336, Result status: Final result
Ordering provider: Khalily, Cyna, MD 06/24/20 0919 Order status: Completed
Resulted by: Reedy, Harold W. Jr., MD Filed by: Interface, Rad Results Enterprise In 06/24/20 1338
Performed: 06/24/20 1114 - 06/24/20 1314 Accession number: [PHONE-1895]
Resulting lab: BAPTIST HEALTH RADIOLOGY
Narrative:
MRI SHOULDER LEFT ARTHROGRAM
CLINICAL INDICATION: M25.512; M25.512-Pain in left shoulder;
G89.29-Other chronic pain. COMPARISON: [DOB-1361]
PROCEDURE: Multiple planar images of the left shoulder were acquired in
a high field strength magnet. Several pulse sequences were used to
acquire the study. IV gadolinium was not administered. However, the
patient was injected intra-articular prior to the exam. FINDINGS: A small portion of the injection was extra-articular, thus
accounting for fluid in the adjacent musculature. There is no evidence of a tear of the rotator cuff. There is signal in the superior labrum, concerning for an anterior
superior labral tear. No adjacent soft tissue mass is seen. No acute fracture.
Impression:
1. Partial extra-articular injection. 2. There is signal within the superior labrum anteriorly concerning for
a small anterior labral tear. 3. No evidence of a rotator cuff tear. This report was finalized on 6/24/2020 1:36 PM by Dr. Harold Reedy, MD. Testing Performed By
Lab - Abbreviation Name Director Address Valid Date Range

Please click epichttp://appointments[here] to view more details about your appointment and 
check in online.
You can use eCheck-In to update your information online before you arrive.
Your safety is our first priority at Baptist Health. If you are experiencing symptoms you feel 
may be related to the COVID-19 virus such as fever greater than 100, acute respiratory 
symptoms (cough and/or shortness of air) or a sudden loss of smell or taste or do not feel 
comfortable coming into an office setting, you may access e-visit and video visit options 
through Baptist Health MyChart. For the health and safety of our patients, all patients and 
visitors will be asked to arrive wearing a mask. Visitors are not permitted with the exception of 
patients under age 18 - one parent may accompany the patient. Dependent patients who 
require assistance and hospice or end-of-life patients may bring one caregiver. All visitors will 
be screened, which may include having a temperature taken. Please check with your provider 
if you have questions. Thank you for trusting Baptist Health with your care!
To schedule a telemedicine appointment, you can go epichttp://evisit[here] to complete an evisit questionnaire online, or go epichttp://apptmake[here] to schedule a video visit.

CONTRAINDICATIONS: PATIENT MUST BE OFF ANY MEDICATIONS THAT CONTAIN GLUCOPHAGE
(METFORMIN), FOR 48 HOURS AFTER ANY INTRAVENOUS CONTRAST EXAMINATION. CHECK WITH
YOUR PHYSICIAN OR PHARMACIST IF UNSURE IF YOUR MEDICATIONS CONTAIN THESE DRUGS.
Your doctor has scheduled you for an x-ray examination that requires an injection of contrast into your blood stream. The
contrast provides the radiologist (A medical doctor who specializes in x-ray procedures and diagnosis) more information than
with x-ray done without the contrast. The contrast may make you feel slightly warm or flushed for a few seconds. This feeling
usually goes away quickly. As with any drug, some patients may be allergic to the contrast. The most common allergic
reaction is itching and hives. Occasionally, (Approx. 1 in 1,000) a patient will develop a more serious reaction, such as
impaired breathing or heart beat irregularities. These reactions may require treatment, which is immediately available in the xray department. Very rarely, (Approx. 1 in 120,000 or less) the contrast reaction can be so severe that death results. There is
no means of testing you for an allergic reaction before injecting the contrast.

1. CONSENT TO DIAGNOSTIC TESTS, PROCEDURES, & MEDICALTREATMENT:| voluntarily consent to (and consent on behalf of any
child born to me while hospitalized) care involving routine diagnostic tests, procedures and medical treatment as ordered by my
admitting and treating physicians, including their assistants or designees. | consent to receive medical care through the use of
telehealth and/or remote patient monitoring. |further consent to the interpretation of diagnostic studies from an off-site location using
telehealth technologies. | consent to medical, nursing, allied health and other students observing and participating in my care under the
supervision of a qualified professional. | consent to photographic recordings or reproducible images during the surgical, medical, and/or
diagnostic procedure(s) and their use for identification, scientific, educational, or research purposes. |also consent to testing for
communicable and blood-borne infectious diseases, such as hepatitis, tuberculosis, and the human immunodeficiency virus (HIV), if a
provider orders testing for diagnostic purposes or if there has been an exposure to healthcare personnel. | have been given no
guarantees about the results that may be obtained from my care.
2. PHYSICIANS AND OTHER HEALTH CARE PROVIDERS WHO ARE NOT EMPLOYEES OF THE HOSPITAL: Physicians and certified
registered nurse anesthetists rendering medical treatment to patients in the hospital are not employed by the Hospital. In most
situations, advanced practitioners, including but not limited to, nurse practitioners, physician assistants, surgical assistants and
psychologists, providing treatment to patients inthe Hospital are not employed by the Hospital. You may receive a separate bill for the
services of these providers, or the Hospital bill you receive may have separate charges for services of such providers. Charges for such
services are established by the providers.
3. FINANCIAL AGREEMENT AND ASSIGNMENT OF BENEFITS:| agree that | am responsible for payment of my Hospital bill. Payment
of any portion of my bill not covered by a third party payor is due upon discharge from the Hospital unless the Hospital has agreed to
other arrangements. | agree to the assignment of all third party payor benefits to the Hospital and to any health care provider rendering
services to me. | acknowledge and agree that Hospital is not required to accept assignment of any third party payor benefits, in which
case, | may receive a bill from Hospital for the full amount of charges related to any care or treatment provided to me or my guarantee by
Hospital and | agree to pay the Hospital and other health care providers for such charges. Moreover, |understand that Hospital may
accept payment from third party payors with whom itdoes not have a written signed agreement and that such acceptance of payment
does not constitute acceptance by Hospital of any reimbursement rates established by such third party payors and that | may receive a
bill from Hospital for the difference between the rate paid by such third party payors and the Hospital’s charges. | agree to pay the
Hospital and other health care providers for all charges for services that are not covered or paid by any third party payor regardless of the
reason, including but not limited to a determination by any third party payor that such services are not covered services or medically
necessary. To the extent |am a Medicare or Medicaid beneficiary, | certify that any information | provide inapplying for payment under
Title XVIII (Medicare) or Title XIX (Medicaid) of the Social Security Act is correct. | request payment of authorized benefits to be made on
my behalf to Baptist Health facility or to any health care provider rendering services to me by the Medicare or Medicaid program. |
hereby irrevocably appoint Hospital as my authorized representative to pursue any claims, penalties, and administrative and/or legal
remedies on my behalf for collection against any responsible payer, employer-sponsored medical benefit plans, third party liability
carrier or, any other responsible third party for any and all benefits due me for the payment of charges associated with my treatment.
This assignment shall not be construed as an obligation of the Hospital to pursue any such right of recovery. | agree to take all actions
necessary to assist Hospital in collecting payment from any such third party payer. | understand that after reasonable notice, any
unpaid account may be turned over to a collection agency and/or attorney for collection. Should it be necessary for the Hospital to
pursue collection, | agree to pay all reasonable collection costs, including court costs and attorney’s fees incurred by the Hospital in
collecting my account. Pursuant to the Fair Credit Reporting Act (15 USC §1681b(a)(2)), | authorize a credit reporting agency engaged
by Hospital or its collection agents to release to the Hospital or any of its representatives or affiliates, my consumer report. |
understand that the purpose of this authorization and request is to obtain my consumer report, which may be used to determine the
availability of or the need for financial assistance, charity care, or insurance coverage for me and may be used for billing and collection
purposes related to payment for services provided to me. | understand this authorization is valid until my Hospital account for all
services is closed.
4. OUTPATIENT MEDICARE PATIENTS: Medicare does not cover prescription drugs with only a few exceptions. | acknowledge that |am
responsible for any drugs furnished to me while an outpatient that meet Medicare’s definition of a prescription drug. These drugs are
also referred to as self-administered but they may be administered to me by Hospital personnel. Hospital may bill Medicare patients or

CONSENT TO WIRELESS TELEPHONE CALLS: | hereby authorize Hospital and all third parties, including clinical providers who have

CONSENT TO EMAIL OR TEXT USAGE FOR HEALTHCARE COMMUNICATIONS: If at any time | provide an email address at which |
may be contacted, | consent to receiving healthcare communication at that email from Hospital. If at any time | provide a cellular

the Hospital. Hospital participates in Care Everywhere, an electronic exchange of patient data, for continuity of care. You may choose to
opt out of Care Everywhere by providing a written request to the Health Information Management Department.
PROMPT DISCHARGE (INPATIENTS ONLY): | agree to prompt discharge following an order that medical treatment is completed and
that continued hospitalization is unnecessary. Late discharges may be subject to additional room charges, which may not be covered
by insurance.

· M25.512,G89.29 (ICD-10-CM) - Chronic left shoulder pain
PREVENTING SURGICAL SITE INFECTIONS
· Call 911 or have someone take you to the Emergency Department if you have any of the following: · Sudden numbness or weakness of your face, arm or leg especially on one side of the body
· Sudden confusion, difficulty speaking or trouble understanding
· Changes in your vision or loss of sight in one eye
· Sudden severe headache with no known cause

It is important to seek emergency care right away if you have further stroke symptoms. If you get
emergency help quickly, the powerful clot-dissolving medicines can reduce the disabilities caused by a
stroke. 
For more information:
American Stroke Association
1-888-4-STROKE
www.strokeassociation.org
IF YOU SMOKE OR USE TOBACCO PLEASE READ THE FOLLOWING:
Why is smoking bad for me?
Smoking increases the risk of heart disease, lung disease, vascular disease, stroke, and cancer. If you
smoke, STOP!
For more information:
Quit Now Kentucky
1-800-QUIT-NOW
https://kentucky.quitlogix.org/en-US/
If you feel like life is too tough and are thinking of suicide or injuring yourself, get help right away! · Call or text 988 to speak to someone. Discharge

Instructions for After Discharge
Patient Instructions
None · Call 911 or have someone take you to the Emergency Department if you have any of the following: · Sudden numbness or weakness of your face, arm or leg especially on one side of the body
· Sudden confusion, difficulty speaking or trouble understanding
· Changes in your vision or loss of sight in one eye
· Sudden severe headache with no known cause
· Sudden dizziness, trouble walking, loss of balance or coordination
It is important to seek emergency care right away if you have further stroke symptoms. If you get
emergency help quickly, the powerful clot-dissolving medicines can reduce the disabilities caused by a
stroke. 
For more information:
American Stroke Association
1-888-4-STROKE
www.strokeassociation.org

Subjective
Patient is a 36-year-old female who presents the emergency department for bilateral leg swelling. The patient had
surgery on her cervical spine on Monday, at Norton Hospital. She had a spinal fusion at the C3-C4 level. The patient
states that she has had pain with her surgery and has been recovering otherwise well. She does state that her leg
swelling started a couple days ago. She is not had any pain in the back of her calves or her legs at all but states that
they have never been swollen like this before. The patient denies any recent fever, chills, nausea, vomiting, shortness
of breath or dysuria. She has no additional symptoms or complaints at this time. She states that she told her surgeon
about the symptoms and was sent to the emergency department to rule out a DVT. Review of Systems
Constitutional: Negative for activity change, appetite change, chills, diaphoresis, fatigue and fever. HENT: Negative for congestion, postnasal drip, rhinorrhea, sinus pressure, sinus pain, sneezing, sore throat and
tinnitus. 
Eyes: Negative for photophobia, pain, discharge, redness and itching. Respiratory: Negative for apnea, cough, choking, chest tightness, shortness of breath, wheezing and stridor. Cardiovascular: Positive for leg swelling. Negative for chest pain and palpitations. Gastrointestinal: Negative for abdominal distention, abdominal pain, constipation, diarrhea, nausea and vomiting. Genitourinary: Negative for difficulty urinating and flank pain. Musculoskeletal: Negative for arthralgias and back pain. Neurological: Negative for dizziness, facial asymmetry, light-headedness and headaches. Psychiatric/Behavioral: Negative for agitation, behavioral problems, confusion and decreased concentration. Narrative:
Among patients with dyspnea, NT-proBNP is highly sensitive for the detection of acute congestive heart failure. In addition NT- proBNP of <300 pg/ml effectively rules out acute congestive heart failure with 99% negative predictive value. Results may be falsely decreased if patient taking Biotin.

Resulting lab: BAPTIST HEALTH RADIOLOGY
Narrative:
EXAMINATION: US VENOUS DOPPLER LOWER EXTREMITY BILATERAL (DUPLEX)- CLINICAL INDICATION: recent surgery, bl leg swelling
TECHNIQUE: Multiplanar gray scale and Doppler vascular sonographic
imaging of the deep veins of BILATERAL lower extremity, without and with
compression. COMPARISON: NONE 
FINDINGS: 
Deep Veins: The visualized deep veins demonstrate flow and are
compressible. No evidence of deep venous thrombosis. Superficial veins: Unremarkable. Saphenofemoral junction is patent
without thrombus. Soft tissues: Unremarkable.
Impression:
No DVT.

Our
LSee
mission is to provide you with high quality individualized care that
A
is safe and effective to meet your
healthcare needs. We will provide you with education and instruction to help with your pain
management/recovery, and we will strive for this to be a successfulprocess. This needs to be a team effort to
include not only your care providers at the hospital, but you, the patient.
Our care goals are:
e To work together in providing your treatment in a respectful manner.
e To help you meet the goals of your treatment plan.
e To maintain a safe environment for thedelivery of health care services at our hospital.
e To protect the safety and well-being of all patients, visitors, staff and care providers.
We value you as a patient and want to continue to provide you with high quality safe care and service. To do so,
we need to outline the expectations that will foster an effective provider- patient relationship.
The following expectations are required to maintain an effective provider- patient relationship.
Patient Behavior Expectations
e | will comply with the medical treatment plan and recommendations developed between my provider
and me.
e | will treat my care providers with dignity and respect, and | expect to be treated with dignity and respect.
e | understand that profanity, physical or verbal abuse, or any other form of violence against anyone will
not be allowed or tolerated.
e | understand that unauthorized and/or illegal drugs are not allowed in the hospital.
e For my safety, |will not leave the nursing unit where |am a patient during my treatment unless
accompanied by hospital personnel for diagnostic procedures or treatments.
e If leave the unit, itwill be considered going against medical advice and | maybe discharged from the
hospital. For any future medical care, |would need to consult my primary care MD/Provider or go to an
Emergency Room for further evaluation. Hospital staff have the right not to re-admit me for further care
unless an emergency situation exists.
