. CONSENT TO DIAGNOSTIC TESTS, PROCEDURES, & MEDICALTREATMENT:| voluntarily consent to (and consent on behalf of any
child born to me while hospitalized) care involving routine diagnostic tests, procedures and medical treatment as ordered by my admitting and
treating physicians, including their assistants or designees. In the case of diagnostic studies, | consent to the interpretation of the studies at an
off-site location through the use of telehealth. | consent to medical, nursing, allied health and other students observing and participating in my
care under the supervision of a qualified professional. | consent to photographic recordings or reproducible images during the surgical,
medical, and/or diagnostic procedure(s) and their use for identification, scientific, educational, or research purposes. | also consent to testing
for communicable and blood-borne infectious diseases, such as hepatitis, tuberculosis, and the human immunodeficiency virus (HIV), if a
provider orders testing for diagnostic purposes or if there has been an exposure to healthcare personnel. | have been given no guarantees
about the results that may be obtained from my care.
. PHYSICIANS AND OTHER HEALTH CARE PROVIDERS WHO ARE NOT EMPLOYEES OF THE HOSPITAL: Physicians and
certified registered nurse anesthetists rendering medical treatment to patients in the hospital are not employed by the Hospital. In most
situations, advanced practitioners, including but not limited to, nurse practitioners, physician assistants, surgical assistants and
psychologists, providing treatment to patients in the Hospital are not employed by the Hospital. You may receive a separate bill for the
services of these providers, or the Hospital bill you receive may have separate charges for services of such providers. Charges for such
services are established by the providers.
. CONSENT TO USE AND DISCLOSURE OF INFORMATION:| authorize the Hospital and the health care providers on its medical staff
to use and disclose information about me to carry out treatment, payment and healthcare operations. Examples of such uses and
disclosures include, but are not limited to, providing information to: (a) any person, company, or entity (such as HMOs, insurance
companies, workers’ compensation carriers, my employer, the Medicare and Medicaid programs and their intermediaries and review
organizations, and any other payor or its review organization or third party administrator) that is or may be liable for paying any claim for
benefits arising out of services provided to me on an inpatient or outpatient basis, or for any ambulance services provided for my transport
to or from the Hospital; (b) any health care provider or ambulance company providing services for me; (c) any providers who may be
providing follow-up care to me after discharge such as home health agencies, nursing homes, physicians, and other health care facilities to
which the patient may be transferred; (d) any licensing or accrediting organizations necessary for the Hospital to obtain or maintain
licensure or accreditation; (e) to providers or entities for the purposes of conducting medical audits, utilization reviews, or quality assurance
reviews; and (f) any other persons or entities described in the Hospital’s Notice of Privacy Practices. This consent includes the release of
medical records and billing information related to drug-related conditions, alcoholism, psychological and psychiatric conditions, and/or
communicable or blood-borne infectious diseases, such as hepatitis, tuberculosis, and the human immunodeficiency virus (HIV). | also
consent to any third party payor or its review organization paying for my care to discuss my plan of treatment for utilization review purposes. |
also authorize the release of my social security number to the manufacturer of any implanted medical device in accordance with the Medical
Device Tracking Act.
. FINANCIAL AGREEMENT AND ASSIGNMENT OF BENEFITS:| agree that | am responsible for payment of myHospital bill. Payment
of any portion of my bill not covered by a third party payor is due upon discharge from the Hospital unless the Hospital has agreed to other
arrangements. | agree to the assignment of all third party payor benefits to the Hospital and to any health care provider rendering services to
me. | agree to pay the Hospital and other health care providers for all charges for services that are not covered or paid by any third party
payor regardless of the reason, including but not limited to a determination by any third party payor that such services are not covered
services or medically necessary. To the extent | am a Medicare or Medicaid beneficiary, | certify that any information | provide in applying for
payment under Title XVIII (Medicare) or Title XIX (Medicaid) of the Social Security Act is correct. | request payment of authorized benefits to be
made on my behalf to Baptist Health facility or to any health care provider rendering services to me by the Medicare or Medicaid program. |
hereby irrevocably appoint Hospital as my authorized representative to pursue any claims, penalties, and administrative and/or legal
remedies on my behalf for collection against any responsible payer, employer-sponsored medical benefit plans, third party liability carrier or,
any other responsible third party for any and all benefits due me for the payment of charges associated with my treatment. This assignment
shall not be construed as an obligation of the Hospital to pursue any such right of recovery. | agree to take all actions necessary to assist

5. OUTPATIENT MEDICARE PATIENTS: Medicare does notcover prescription drugs with only a few exceptions. According to Medicare
regulations, | acknowledge that | am responsible for any drugs furnished to me while an outpatient that meet Medicare’s definition of a
prescription drug. These drugs are also referred to as self-administered but they may be administered to me by Hospital personnel.
Medicare requires Hospital to bill Medicare patients or other third party payers for these drugs. Medicare Part D beneficiaries may submit a
paper claim to the Medicare Part D Plan for possible reimbursement of these drugs in accordance with Medicare Drug Plan enrollment
materials.

[DOB-1655] - ED in Baptist Health Corbin Emergency Department (continued)
Edema is when you have too much fluid in your body or under your skin. Edema may 
make your legs, feet, and ankles swell up. Swelling is also common in looser tissues, 
like around your eyes. This is a common condition. It gets more common as you get 
older. There are many possible causes of edema. Eating too much salt (sodium) and 
being on your feet or sitting for a long time can cause edema in your legs, feet, and 
ankles. Hot weather may make edema worse.
Edema is usually painless. Your skin may look swollen or shiny.
Follow these instructions at home: · Keep the swollen body part raised (elevated) above the level of your heart when you are sitting or lying down. · Do not sit still or stand for a long time. · Do not wear tight clothes. Do not wear garters on your upper legs. · Exercise your legs. This can help the swelling go down. · Wear elastic bandages or support stockings as told by your doctor. · Eat a low-salt (low-sodium) diet to reduce fluid as told by your doctor. · Depending on the cause of your swelling, you may need to limit how much fluid you drink (fluid restriction). · Take over-the-counter and prescription medicines only as told by your doctor.
Contact a doctor if: · Treatment is not working. · You have heart, liver, or kidney disease and have symptoms of edema. · You have sudden and unexplained weight gain.
Get help right away if: · You have shortness of breath or chest pain. · You cannot breathe when you lie down. · You have pain, redness, or warmth in the swollen areas. · You have heart, liver, or kidney disease and get edema all of a sudden. · You have a fever and your symptoms get worse all of a sudden.
Summary
· Edema is when you have too much fluid in your body or under your skin. · Edema may make your legs, feet, and ankles swell up. Swelling is also common in looser tissues, like around 
your eyes. · Raise (elevate) the swollen body part above the level of your heart when you are sitting or lying down. · Follow your doctor's instructions about diet and how much fluid you can drink (fluid restriction).
This information is not intended to replace advice given to you by your health care provider. Make sure you discuss 
any questions you have with your health care provider.
More Information

Elsevier Interactive Patient Education © 2019 Elsevier Inc. · Call 911 or have someone take you to the Emergency Department if you have any of the following: · Sudden numbness or weakness of your face, arm or leg especially on one side of the body
· Sudden confusion, difficulty speaking or trouble understanding
· Changes in your vision or loss of sight in one eye
· Sudden severe headache with no known cause
· Sudden dizziness, trouble walking, loss of balance or coordination
It is important to seek emergency care right away if you have further stroke symptoms. If you get
emergency help quickly, the powerful clot-dissolving medicines can reduce the disabilities caused by a
stroke. 
For more information:
American Stroke Association
1-888-4-STROKE
www.strokeassociation.org
IF YOU SMOKE OR USE TOBACCO PLEASE READ THE FOLLOWING:
Why is smoking bad for me?
Smoking increases the risk of heart disease, lung disease, vascular disease, stroke, and cancer. If you
smoke, STOP!
For more information:
Quit Now Kentucky
1-800-QUIT-NOW
https://kentucky.quitlogix.org/en-US/
If you feel like life is too tough and are thinking of suicide or injuring yourself, get help right away! · Call or text 988 to speak to someone. Screenings - 01/24/20 1426

INPATIENT AND OUTPATIENT CONDITIONS AND CONSENTS CSN: 91066360989 ADMIT DATE:
1. CONSENT TO DIAGNOSTIC TESTS, PROCEDURES, & MEDICALTREATMENT:| voluntarily consent to (and consent on behalf of any child born to
me while hospitalized) care involving routine diagnostic tests, procedures and medical treatment as ordered by my admitting and treating
physicians, including their assistants or designees. In the case of diagnostic studies, | consent to the interpretation of the studies at an off-site
location through the use of telehealth. | consent to medical, nursing, allied health and other students observing and participating in my care
under the supervision of a qualified professional. | consent to photographic recordings or reproducible images during the surgical, medical,
and/or diagnostic procedure(s) and their use for identification, scientific, educational, or research purposes. | also consent to testing for
communicable and blood-borne infectious diseases, such as hepatitis, tuberculosis, and the human immunodeficiency virus (HIV), if a provider
orders testing for diagnostic purposes or if there has been an exposure to healthcare personnel. | have been given no guarantees about the
results that may be obtained from my care.
2. PHYSICIANS AND OTHER HEALTH CARE PROVIDERS WHO ARE NOT EMPLOYEES OF THE HOSPITAL: Physicians and certified registered
nurse anesthetists rendering medical treatment to patients in the hospital are not employed by the Hospital. In most situations, advanced
practitioners, including but not limited to, nurse practitioners, physician assistants, surgical assistants and psychologists, providing
treatment to patients in the Hospital are not employed by the Hospital. You may receive a separate bill for the services of these providers, or
the Hospital bill you receive may have separate charges for services of such providers. Charges for such services are established by the
providers.
3. CONSENT TO USE AND DISCLOSURE OF INFORMATION: |authorize the Hospital and the health care providers on its medical staff to use
and disclose information about me to carry out treatment, payment and healthcare operations. Examples of such uses and disclosures
include, but are not limited to, providing information to: (a) any person, company, or entity (Such as HMOs, insurance companies, workers’
compensation carriers, my employer, the Medicare and Medicaid programs and their intermediaries and review organizations, and any
other payor or its review organization or third party administrator) that is or may be liable for paying any claim for benefits arising out of
services provided to me on an inpatient or outpatient basis, or for any ambulance services provided for my transport to or from the Hospital;
(b) any health care provider or ambulance company providing services for me; (c) any providers who may be providing follow-up care to me
after discharge such as home health agencies, nursing homes, physicians, and other health care facilities to which the patient may be
transferred; (d) any licensing or accrediting organizations necessary for the Hospital to obtain or maintain licensure or accreditation; (e) to
providers or entities for the purposes of conducting medical audits, utilization reviews, or quality assurance reviews; and (f) any other
persons or entities described in the Hospital’s Notice of Privacy Practices. This consent includes the release of medical records and billing
information related to drug-related conditions, alcoholism, psychological and psychiatric conditions, and/or communicable or blood-borne
infectious diseases, such as hepatitis, tuberculosis, and the human immunodeficiency virus (HIV). | also consent to any third party payor or
its review organization paying for my care to discuss my plan of treatment for utilization review purposes. | also authorize the release of my
social security number to the manufacturer of any implanted medical device in accordance with the Medical Device Tracking Act.
4. FINANCIAL AGREEMENT ANDASSIGNMENT OF BENEFITS:| agree that | am responsible for payment of my Hospital bill. Payment of any
portion of my bill not covered by a third party payor is due upon discharge from the Hospital unless the Hospital has agreed to other
arrangements. | agree to the assignment of all third party payor benefits to the Hospital and to any health care provider rendering services to
me. | agree to pay the Hospital and other health care providers for all charges for services that are not covered or paid by any third party
payor regardless of the reason, including but not limited to a determination by any third party payor that such services are not covered
services or medically necessary. To the extent | am a Medicare or Medicaid beneficiary, | certify that any information | provide in applying for
payment under Title XVIII (Medicare) or Title XIX (Medicaid) of the Social Security Act is correct. | request payment of authorized benefits to be
made on my behalf to Baptist Health facility or to any health care provider rendering services to me by the Medicare or Medicaid program. |
hereby irrevocably appoint Hospital as my authorized representative to pursue any claims, penalties, and administrative and/or legal
remedies on my behalf for collection against any responsible payer, employer-sponsored medical benefit plans, third party liability carrier or,
any other responsible third party for any and all benefits due me for the payment of charges associated with my treatment. This assignment
shall not be construed as an obligation of the Hospital to pursue any such right of recovery. | agree to take all actions necessary to assist
Hospital in collecting payment from any such third party payer. | understand that after reasonable notice, any unpaid account may be turned
over to a collection agency and/or attorney for collection. Should it be necessary for the Hospital to pursue collection, | agree to pay all
reasonable collection costs, including

5. OUTPATIENT MEDICARE PATIENTS: Medicare does not cover prescription drugs with only a few exceptions. According to Medicare
regulations, | acknowledge that | am responsible for any drugs furnished to me while an outpatient that meet Medicare’s definition of a
prescription drug. These drugs are also referred to as self-administered but they may be administered to me by Hospital personnel.
Medicare requires Hospital to bill Medicare patients or other third party payers for these drugs. Medicare Part D beneficiaries may submit a
paper claim to the Medicare Part D Plan for possible reimbursement of these drugs in accordance with Medicare Drug Plan enrollment
materials.
6. HOSPITAL IS NOT RESPONSIBLE FOR LOSS OF PERSONAL ARTICLES: | UNDERSTAND THAT THE HOSPITAL MAINTAINS A SAFE FOR

JEWELRY, GLASSES, DENTURES, DOCUMENTS, CLOTHING, OR OTHER ITEMS OF PERSONAL PROPERTY.
7. SMOKE-FREE ENVIRONMENT: This Hospital maintains a smoke-free environment on its campus. Smoking is prohibited by health care
personnel, patients, and visitors on the Hospital’s campus. | understand that | am to remain in my treatment area at all times. | further
understand that if | leave my treatment area, | will not be under nursing or medical supervision, and further understand that leaving my
treatment area may complicate or interfere with the course of treatment prescribed by my physician.
8. NOTICE OF NONDISCRIMINATION: Hospital programs and activities are available regardless of race, color, national origin, religion, sex
(including gender identity and gender stereotyping), disability or any other status protected by federal, state or local law.

PREVENTING SURGICAL SITE INFECTIONS
· Call 911 or have someone take you to the Emergency Department if you have any of the following: · Sudden numbness or weakness of your face, arm or leg especially on one side of the body
· Sudden confusion, difficulty speaking or trouble understanding
· Changes in your vision or loss of sight in one eye
· Sudden severe headache with no known cause
· Sudden dizziness, trouble walking, loss of balance or coordination
It is important to seek emergency care right away if you have further stroke symptoms. If you get
emergency help quickly, the powerful clot-dissolving medicines can reduce the disabilities caused by a
stroke. 
For more information:
American Stroke Association
1-888-4-STROKE
www.strokeassociation.org
IF YOU SMOKE OR USE TOBACCO PLEASE READ THE FOLLOWING:
Why is smoking bad for me?
Smoking increases the risk of heart disease, lung disease, vascular disease, stroke, and cancer. If you
smoke, STOP!
For more information:
Quit Now Kentucky
1-800-QUIT-NOW
https://kentucky.quitlogix.org/en-US/
If you feel like life is too tough and are thinking of suicide or injuring yourself, get help right away! · Call or text 988 to speak to someone. Discharge
Home or Self Care

through selected cervical disc space levels, utilizing both spin echo
and gradient echo scans technique without contrast administration. COMPARISON: NONE

[DOB-1845] - MR cor cspine wo contrast in BAPTIST HEALTH CORBIN SOUTH IMAGING MRI (continued)
Imaging (continued)
FINDINGS:
Vertebral body height and alignment are intact. Modic type changes are present at the C3/4 level. Marrow signal
otherwise unremarkable throughout. Cervical spinal cord demonstrates no mass, edema, or syrinx. The posterior elements are intact. The prevertebral soft tissues are within normal limits. Cervical medullary junction and visualized portions of posterior fossa
are unremarkable. C2/3: Unremarkable. C3/4: Disc osteophyte complex and small central posterior disc
protrusion. Moderate central canal stenosis. Effacement of the ventral
thecal sac. There is mild right-sided neural foraminal stenosis. C4/5: Unremarkable. C5/6: Unremarkable. C6/7: Unremarkable. C7/T1: Unremarkable.
Impression:
1. Degenerative disc disease and small superimposed disc protrusion at
C3/4 causing moderate central canal and mild right-sided neural
foraminal stenosis. 2. Otherwise unremarkable exam. This report was finalized on 6/22/2019 11:50 AM by Dr. Kevin J Croce, MD.

INPATIENT AND OUTPATIENT CONDITIONS AND CONSENTS CSN: 91063233676 ADMIT DATE:
1. CONSENT TO DIAGNOSTIC TESTS, PROCEDURES, & MEDICALTREATMENT:| voluntarily consent to (and consent on behalf of any child born to
me while hospitalized) care involving routine diagnostic tests, procedures and medical treatment as ordered by my admitting and treating
physicians, including their assistants or designees. In the case of diagnostic studies, | consent to the interpretation of the studies at an off-site
location through the use of telehealth. | consent to medical, nursing, allied health and other students observing and participating in my care
under the supervision of a qualified professional. | consent to photographic recordings or reproducible images during the surgical, medical,
and/or diagnostic procedure(s) and their use for identification, scientific, educational, or research purposes. | also consent to testing for
communicable and blood-borne infectious diseases, such as hepatitis, tuberculosis, and the human immunodeficiency virus (HIV), if a provider
orders testing for diagnostic purposes or if there has been an exposure to healthcare personnel. | have been given no guarantees about the
results that may be obtained from my care.
2. PHYSICIANS AND OTHER HEALTH CARE PROVIDERS WHO ARE NOT EMPLOYEES OF THE HOSPITAL: Physicians and certified registered
nurse anesthetists rendering medical treatment to patients in the hospital are not employed by the Hospital. In most situations, advanced
practitioners, including but not limited to, nurse practitioners, physician assistants, surgical assistants and psychologists, providing
treatment to patients in the Hospital are not employed by the Hospital. You may receive a separate bill for the services of these providers, or
the Hospital bill you receive may have separate charges for services of such providers. Charges for such services are established by the
providers.
3. CONSENT TO USE AND DISCLOSURE OF INFORMATION: |authorize the Hospital and the health care providers on its medical staff to use
and disclose information about me to carry out treatment, payment and healthcare operations. Examples of such uses and disclosures
include, but are not limited to, providing information to: (a) any person, company, or entity (Such as HMOs, insurance companies, workers’
compensation carriers, my employer, the Medicare and Medicaid programs and their intermediaries and review organizations, and any
other payor or its review organization or third party administrator) that is or may be liable for paying any claim for benefits arising out of
services provided to me on an inpatient or outpatient basis, or for any ambulance services provided for my transport to or from the Hospital;
(b) any health care provider or ambulance company providing services for me; (c) any providers who may be providing follow-up care to me
after discharge such as home health agencies, nursing homes, physicians, and other health care facilities to which the patient may be
transferred; (d) any licensing or accrediting organizations necessary for the Hospital to obtain or maintain licensure or accreditation; (e) to
providers or entities for the purposes of conducting medical audits, utilization reviews, or quality assurance reviews; and (f) any other
persons or entities described in the Hospital’s Notice of Privacy Practices. This consent includes the release of medical records and billing
information related to drug-related conditions, alcoholism, psychological and psychiatric conditions, and/or communicable or blood-borne
infectious diseases, such as hepatitis, tuberculosis, and the human immunodeficiency virus (HIV). | also consent to any third party payor or
its review organization paying for my care to discuss my plan of treatment for utilization review purposes. | also authorize the release of my
social security number to the manufacturer of any implanted medical device in accordance with the Medical Device Tracking Act.
4. FINANCIAL AGREEMENT ANDASSIGNMENT OF BENEFITS:| agree that | am responsible for payment of my Hospital bill. Payment of any
portion of my bill not covered by a third party payor is due upon discharge from the Hospital unless the Hospital has agreed to other
arrangements. | agree to the assignment of all third party payor benefits to the Hospital and to any health care provider rendering services to
me. | agree to pay the Hospital and other health care providers for all charges for services that are not covered or paid by any third party
payor regardless of the reason, including but not limited to a determination by any third party payor that such services are not covered
services or medically necessary. To the extent | am a Medicare or Medicaid beneficiary, | certify that any information | provide in applying for
payment under Title XVIII (Medicare) or Title XIX (Medicaid) of the Social Security Act is correct. | request payment of authorized benefits to be
made on my behalf to Baptist Health facility or to any health care provider rendering services to me by the Medicare or Medicaid program. |
hereby irrevocably appoint Hospital as my authorized representative to pursue any claims, penalties, and administrative and/or legal
remedies on my behalf for collection against any responsible payer, employer-sponsored medical benefit plans, third party liability carrier or,
any other responsible third party for any and all benefits due me for the payment of charges associated with my treatment. This assignment
shall not be construed as an obligation of the Hospital to pursue any such right of recovery. | agree to take all actions necessary to assist
Hospital in collecting payment from any such third party payer. | understand that after reasonable notice, any unpaid account may be turned
over to a collection agency and/or attorney for collection. Should it be necessary for the Hospital to pursue collection, | agree to pay all
reasonable collection costs, including

PREVENTING SURGICAL SITE INFECTIONS
· Call 911 or have someone take you to the Emergency Department if you have any of the following: · Sudden numbness or weakness of your face, arm or leg especially on one side of the body
· Sudden confusion, difficulty speaking or trouble understanding
· Changes in your vision or loss of sight in one eye
· Sudden severe headache with no known cause
· Sudden dizziness, trouble walking, loss of balance or coordination
It is important to seek emergency care right away if you have further stroke symptoms. If you get
emergency help quickly, the powerful clot-dissolving medicines can reduce the disabilities caused by a
stroke. 
For more information:
American Stroke Association
1-888-4-STROKE
www.strokeassociation.org
IF YOU SMOKE OR USE TOBACCO PLEASE READ THE FOLLOWING:
Why is smoking bad for me?
Smoking increases the risk of heart disease, lung disease, vascular disease, stroke, and cancer. If you
smoke, STOP!
For more information:
Quit Now Kentucky
1-800-QUIT-NOW
https://kentucky.quitlogix.org/en-US/
If you feel like life is too tough and are thinking of suicide or injuring yourself, get help right away! · Call or text 988 to speak to someone. Discharge
Home or Self Care

Resulting lab: BAPTIST HEALTH RADIOLOGY
Narrative:
EXAMINATION: XR SPINE CERVICAL 2 OR 3 VW- CLINICAL INDICATION: neck pain with radiculopathy; M54.2-Cervicalgia
TECHNIQUE: X-rays of the cervical spine with 3 views. COMPARISON: NONE 
FINDINGS: There is multilevel degenerative change. The alignment is
within normal limits. There is no prevertebral nor other soft tissue
abnormality. The vertebral bodies are normal in height. There is no
fracture, focal lesion or loss of volume. There is no significant disc
space narrowing. The neural foramina appear patent.
Impression:
Unremarkable evaluation of the the cervical spine. This report was finalized on 6/12/2019 2:37 PM by Dr. Lonnie Bargo, MD. Printed on 1/19/23 12:43 PM Page 386

INPATIENT AND OUTPATIENT CONDITIONS AND CONSENTS | |ll | (AAA ll
1. CONSENT TO DIAGNOSTIC TESTS, PROCEDURES, & MEDICALTREATMENT:| voluntarily consent to
(and consent on behalf of any child born to me while hospitalized) care involving routine diagnostic tests, procedures and
medical treatment as ordered by my admitting and treating physicians, including their assistants or designees. In the case of
diagnostic studies, | consent to the interpretation ofthe studies at anoff-site location through the use oftelehealth. |consent to
medical, nursing, allied health and other students observing and participating inmy care under the supervision of a qualified
professional. | consent to photographic recordings or reproducible images during the surgical, medical, and/or diagnostic
procedure(s) and their use for identification, scientific, educational, or research purposes. | also consent to testing for
communicable and blood-borne infectious diseases, such as hepatitis, tuberculosis, and the human immunodeficiency virus
(HIV), ifa provider orders testing for diagnostic purposes or ifthere has been an exposure to healthcare personnel. | have
been given noguarantees about the results that may be obtained from my care.
2. PHYSICIANS AND OTHER HEALTH CARE PROVIDERS WHO ARE NOT EMPLOYEES OF THE
HOSPITAL: Physicians and certified registered nurse anesthetists rendering medical treatment to patients in the
hospital are not employed by the Hospital. In most situations, advanced practitioners, including but not limited to, nurse
practitioners, physician assistants, surgical assistants and psychologists, providing treatment to patients in the Hospital are
not employed bythe Hospital. You may receive a separate bill for the services of these providers, or the Hospital bill you
receive may have separate charges for services of such providers. Charges for such services are established by the
providers.
3. CONSENT TO USE AND DISCLOSURE OF INFORMATION:| authorize the Hospital and the health care
providers onits medical staff to use and disclose information about me to carry out treatment, payment and healthcare
operations. Examples of such uses and disclosures include, but are not limited to, providing information to: (a) any person,
company, or entity (such as HMOs, insurance companies, workers’ compensation carriers, myemployer, the Medicare and
Medicaid programs and their intermediaries and review organizations, and any other payor or itsreview organization or
third party administrator) that is or may be liable for paying any claim for benefits arising out of services provided to me on
an inpatient oroutpatient basis, or for any ambulance services provided for my transport to or from the Hospital; (b) any
health care provider or ambulance company providing services for me; (c) any providers who may beproviding follow-up
care to me after discharge such as home health agencies, nursing homes, physicians, and other health care facilities to
which the patient may be transferred; (d) any licensing or accrediting organizations necessary for the Hospital to obtain or
maintain licensure or accreditation; (e) to providers or entities for the purposes of conducting medical audits, utilization
reviews, or quality assurance reviews; and (f) any other persons or entities described inthe Hospital’s Notice of Privacy
Practices. This consent includes the release of medical records and billing information related to drug-related conditions,
alcoholism, psychological and psychiatric conditions, and/or communicable or blood-borne infectious diseases, such as
hepatitis, tuberculosis, and the human immunodeficiency virus (HIV). | also consent to any third party payor or its review
organization paying for my care to discuss my plan of treatment for utilization review purposes.| also authorize the release
of my social security number to the manufacturer of any implanted medical device in accordance with the Medical Device
Tracking Act.
4. FINANCIAL AGREEMENT AND ASSIGNMENT OF BENEFITS:| agree that |am responsible for payment of
my Hospital bill. Payment of any portion of my bill not covered bya third party payor is due upon discharge from the
Hospital unless the Hospital has agreed to other arrangements. | agree to the assignment of all third party payor benefits to
the Hospital and to any health care provider rendering services to me. | agree to pay the Hospital and other health care
providers for all charges for services that are not covered or paid by any third party payor regardless ofthe reason,
including but not limited to a determination by any third party payor that such services are not covered services or
medically necessary. To the extent | ama Medicare or Medicaid beneficiary, | certify that any information | provide in
applying for payment under Title XVIII (Medicare) orTitle XIX (Medicaid) of the Social Security Act is correct. | request
payment of authorized benefits to bemade on mybehalf to Baptist Health facility or to any health care provider rendering
services to me by the Medicare or Medicaid program. | hereby irrevocably appoint Hospital as my authorized
representative to pursue any claims, penalties, and administrative and/or legal remedies onmy behalf for collection against
any responsible payer, employer-sponsored medical benefit plans, third party liability carrier or, any other responsible third
party for any and all benefits due me for the payment of charges associated with my treatment. This assignment shall not
beconstrued as an obligation of the Hospital to pursue any such right of recovery. | agree to take allactions necessary to
assist Hospital in collecting payment from any such third party payer. | understand that after reasonable notice, any unpaid
account may beturned over to a collection agency and/or attorney for collection. Should it be necessary for the Hospital to
pursue collection, | agree to pay all reasonable collection costs, including court costs and

PREVENTING SURGICAL SITE INFECTIONS
· Call 911 or have someone take you to the Emergency Department if you have any of the following: · Sudden numbness or weakness of your face, arm or leg especially on one side of the body
· Sudden confusion, difficulty speaking or trouble understanding
· Changes in your vision or loss of sight in one eye
· Sudden severe headache with no known cause
· Sudden dizziness, trouble walking, loss of balance or coordination
It is important to seek emergency care right away if you have further stroke symptoms. If you get
emergency help quickly, the powerful clot-dissolving medicines can reduce the disabilities caused by a
stroke. 
For more information:
American Stroke Association
1-888-4-STROKE
www.strokeassociation.org
IF YOU SMOKE OR USE TOBACCO PLEASE READ THE FOLLOWING:
Why is smoking bad for me?
Smoking increases the risk of heart disease, lung disease, vascular disease, stroke, and cancer. If you
smoke, STOP!
For more information:
Quit Now Kentucky
1-800-QUIT-NOW
https://kentucky.quitlogix.org/en-US/
If you feel like life is too tough and are thinking of suicide or injuring yourself, get help right away! · Call or text 988 to speak to someone. Discharge
Home or Self Care

Imaging (continued)
Gadolinium was not administered. FINDINGS:
The diffusion weighted images show no focal signal abnormality. There is
no evidence of acute ischemia
The remaining pulse sequences show no mass, hemorrhage, or midline
shift. The ventricles, cisterns, and sulci are unremarkable. There is no
hydrocephalus.
The sagittal view show no sellar or parasellar mass. There is no tectal or pineal lesion. Coronal imaging demonstrates a symmetric appearance of the visualized
portions of the optic nerves and extraocular muscles.
Impression:
No definitive source for the patient's symptoms. No mass hemorrhage or midline shift

1. CONSENT TO DIAGNOSTIC TESTS, PROCEDURES, & MEDICALTREATMENT:| voluntarily consent to (and consent on behalf of any child born to
me while hospitalized) care involving routine diagnostic tests, procedures and medical treatment as ordered by my admitting and treating
physicians, including their assistants or designees. In the case of diagnostic studies, | consent to the interpretation of the studies at an off-site
location through the use of telehealth. | consent to medical, nursing, allied health and other students observing and participating in my care
under the supervision of a qualified professional. | consent to photographic recordings or reproducible images during the surgical, medical,
and/or diagnostic procedure(s) and their use for identification, scientific, educational, or research purposes. | also consent to testing for
communicable and blood-borne infectious diseases, such as hepatitis, tuberculosis, and the human immunodeficiency virus (HIV), if a provider
orders testing for diagnostic purposes or if there has been an exposure to healthcare personnel. | have been given no guarantees about the
results that may be obtained from my care.
2. PHYSICIANS AND OTHER HEALTH CARE PROVIDERS WHO ARE NOT EMPLOYEES OF THE HOSPITAL: Physicians and certified registered
nurse anesthetists rendering medical treatment to patients in the hospital are not employed by the Hospital. In most situations, advanced
practitioners, including but not limited to, nurse practitioners, physician assistants, surgical assistants and psychologists, providing
treatment to patients in the Hospital are not employed by the Hospital. You may receive a separate bill for the services of these providers, or
the Hospital bill you receive may have separate charges for services of such providers. Charges for such services are established by the
providers.
3. CONSENT TO USE AND DISCLOSURE OF INFORMATION: |authorize the Hospital and the health care providers on its medical staff to use
and disclose information about me to carry out treatment, payment and healthcare operations. Examples of such uses and disclosures
include, but are not limited to, providing information to: (a) any person, company, or entity (Such as HMOs, insurance companies, workers’
compensation carriers, my employer, the Medicare and Medicaid programs and their intermediaries and review organizations, and any
other payor or its review organization or third party administrator) that is or may be liable for paying any claim for benefits arising out of
services provided to me on an inpatient or outpatient basis, or for any ambulance services provided for my transport to or from the Hospital;
(b) any health care provider or ambulance company providing services for me; (c) any providers who may be providing follow-up care to me
after discharge such as home health agencies, nursing homes, physicians, and other health care facilities to which the patient may be
transferred; (d) any licensing or accrediting organizations necessary for the Hospital to obtain or maintain licensure or accreditation; (e) to
providers or entities for the purposes of conducting medical audits, utilization reviews, or quality assurance reviews; and (f) any other
persons or entities described in the Hospital’s Notice of Privacy Practices. This consent includes the release of medical records and billing
information related to drug-related conditions, alcoholism, psychological and psychiatric conditions, and/or communicable or blood-borne
infectious diseases, such as hepatitis, tuberculosis, and the human immunodeficiency virus (HIV). | also consent to any third party payor or
its review organization paying for my care to discuss my plan of treatment for utilization review purposes. | also authorize the release of my
social security number to the manufacturer of any implanted medical device in accordance with the Medical Device Tracking Act.
4. FINANCIAL AGREEMENT ANDASSIGNMENT OF BENEFITS:| agree that | am responsible for payment of my Hospital bill. Payment of any
portion of my bill not covered by a third party payor is due upon discharge from the Hospital unless the Hospital has agreed to other
arrangements. | agree to the assignment of all third party payor benefits to the Hospital and to any health care provider rendering services to
me. | agree to pay the Hospital and other health care providers for all charges for services that are not covered or paid by any third party
payor regardless of the reason, including but not limited to a determination by any third party payor that such services are not covered
services or medically necessary. To the extent | am a Medicare or Medicaid beneficiary, | certify that any information | provide in applying for
payment under Title XVIII (Medicare) or Title XIX (Medicaid) of the Social Security Act is correct. | request payment of authorized benefits to be
made on my behalf to Baptist Health facility or to any health care provider rendering services to me by the Medicare or Medicaid program. |
hereby irrevocably appoint Hospital as my authorized representative to pursue any claims, penalties, and administrative and/or legal
remedies on my behalf for collection against any responsible payer, employer-sponsored medical benefit plans, third party liability carrier or,
any other responsible third party for any and all benefits due me for the payment of charges associated with my treatment. This assignment
shall not be construed as an obligation of the Hospital to pursue any such right of recovery. | agree to take all actions necessary to assist
Hospital in collecting payment from any such third party payer. | understand that after reasonable notice, any unpaid account may be turned
over to a collection agency and/or attorney for collection. Should it be necessary for the Hospital to pursue collection, | agree to pay all
reasonable collection costs, including

5. OUTPATIENT MEDICARE PATIENTS: Medicare does not cover prescription drugs with only a few exceptions. According to Medicare
regulations, | acknowledge that | am responsible for any drugs furnished to me while an outpatient that meet Medicare’s definition of a

paper claim to the Medicare Part D Plan for possible reimbursement of these drugs in accordance with Medicare Drug Plan enrollment

· G44.89 (ICD-10-CM) - Headache syndrome
PREVENTING SURGICAL SITE INFECTIONS
· Call 911 or have someone take you to the Emergency Department if you have any of the following: · Sudden numbness or weakness of your face, arm or leg especially on one side of the body
· Sudden confusion, difficulty speaking or trouble understanding
· Changes in your vision or loss of sight in one eye
· Sudden severe headache with no known cause
· Sudden dizziness, trouble walking, loss of balance or coordination

It is important to seek emergency care right away if you have further stroke symptoms. If you get
emergency help quickly, the powerful clot-dissolving medicines can reduce the disabilities caused by a
stroke. 
For more information:
American Stroke Association
1-888-4-STROKE
www.strokeassociation.org
IF YOU SMOKE OR USE TOBACCO PLEASE READ THE FOLLOWING:
Why is smoking bad for me?
Smoking increases the risk of heart disease, lung disease, vascular disease, stroke, and cancer. If you
smoke, STOP!
For more information:
Quit Now Kentucky
1-800-QUIT-NOW
https://kentucky.quitlogix.org/en-US/

Imaging (continued)
FINDINGS:
The diffusion weighted images show no focal signal abnormality. There is
no evidence of acute ischemia
The remaining pulse sequences show no mass, hemorrhage, or midline
shift. The ventricles, cisterns, and sulci are unremarkable. There is no
hydrocephalus.
The sagittal view show no sellar or parasellar mass. There is no tectal or pineal lesion. Coronal imaging demonstrates a symmetric appearance of the visualized
portions of the optic nerves and extraocular muscles.
Impression:
No definitive source for the patient's symptoms. No mass hemorrhage or midline shift

INPATIENT AND OUTPATIENT CONDITIONS AND CONSENTS CSN: 91060286284 ADMIT DATE:
1. CONSENT TO DIAGNOSTIC TESTS, PROCEDURES, & MEDICALTREATMENT:| voluntarily consent to (and consent on behalf of any child born to
me while hospitalized) care involving routine diagnostic tests, procedures and medical treatment as ordered by my admitting and treating
physicians, including their assistants or designees. In the case of diagnostic studies, | consent to the interpretation of the studies at an off-site
location through the use of telehealth. | consent to medical, nursing, allied health and other students observing and participating in my care
under the supervision of a qualified professional. | consent to photographic recordings or reproducible images during the surgical, medical,
and/or diagnostic procedure(s) and their use for identification, scientific, educational, or research purposes. | also consent to testing for
communicable and blood-borne infectious diseases, such as hepatitis, tuberculosis, and the human immunodeficiency virus (HIV), if a provider
orders testing for diagnostic purposes or if there has been an exposure to healthcare personnel. | have been given no guarantees about the
results that may be obtained from my care.
2. PHYSICIANS AND OTHER HEALTH CARE PROVIDERS WHO ARE NOT EMPLOYEES OF THE HOSPITAL: Physicians and certified registered
nurse anesthetists rendering medical treatment to patients in the hospital are not employed by the Hospital. In most situations, advanced
practitioners, including but not limited to, nurse practitioners, physician assistants, surgical assistants and psychologists, providing
treatment to patients in the Hospital are not employed by the Hospital. You may receive a separate bill for the services of these providers, or
the Hospital bill you receive may have separate charges for services of such providers. Charges for such services are established by the
providers.
3. CONSENT TO USE AND DISCLOSURE OF INFORMATION: |authorize the Hospital and the health care providers on its medical staff to use
and disclose information about me to carry out treatment, payment and healthcare operations. Examples of such uses and disclosures
include, but are not limited to, providing information to: (a) any person, company, or entity (Such as HMOs, insurance companies, workers’
compensation carriers, my employer, the Medicare and Medicaid programs and their intermediaries and review organizations, and any
other payor or its review organization or third party administrator) that is or may be liable for paying any claim for benefits arising out of
services provided to me on an inpatient or outpatient basis, or for any ambulance services provided for my transport to or from the Hospital;
(b) any health care provider or ambulance company providing services for me; (c) any providers who may be providing follow-up care to me
after discharge such as home health agencies, nursing homes, physicians, and other health care facilities to which the patient may be
transferred; (d) any licensing or accrediting organizations necessary for the Hospital to obtain or maintain licensure or accreditation; (e) to
providers or entities for the purposes of conducting medical audits, utilization reviews, or quality assurance reviews; and (f) any other
persons or entities described in the Hospital’s Notice of Privacy Practices. This consent includes the release of medical records and billing
information related to drug-related conditions, alcoholism, psychological and psychiatric conditions, and/or communicable or blood-borne
infectious diseases, such as hepatitis, tuberculosis, and the human immunodeficiency virus (HIV). | also consent to any third party payor or
its review organization paying for my care to discuss my plan of treatment for utilization review purposes. | also authorize the release of my
social security number to the manufacturer of any implanted medical device in accordance with the Medical Device Tracking Act.
4. FINANCIAL AGREEMENT ANDASSIGNMENT OF BENEFITS:| agree that | am responsible for payment of my Hospital bill. Payment of any
portion of my bill not covered by a third party payor is due upon discharge from the Hospital unless the Hospital has agreed to other
arrangements. | agree to the assignment of all third party payor benefits to the Hospital and to any health care provider rendering services to
me. | agree to pay the Hospital and other health care providers for all charges for services that are not covered or paid by any third party
payor regardless of the reason, including but not limited to a determination by any third party payor that such services are not covered
services or medically necessary. To the extent | am a Medicare or Medicaid beneficiary, | certify that any information | provide in applying for
payment under Title XVIII (Medicare) or Title XIX (Medicaid) of the Social Security Act is correct. | request payment of authorized benefits to be
made on my behalf to Baptist Health facility or to any health care provider rendering services to me by the Medicare or Medicaid program. |
hereby irrevocably appoint Hospital as my authorized representative to pursue any claims, penalties, and administrative and/or legal
remedies on my behalf for collection against any responsible payer, employer-sponsored medical benefit plans, third party liability carrier or,
any other responsible third party for any and all benefits due me for the payment of charges associated with my treatment. This assignment
shall not be construed as an obligation of the Hospital to pursue any such right of recovery. | agree to take all actions necessary to assist
Hospital in collecting payment from any such third party payer. | understand that after reasonable notice, any unpaid account may be turned
over to a collection agency and/or attorney for collection. Should it be necessary for the Hospital to pursue collection, | agree to pay all
reasonable collection costs, including

5. OUTPATIENT MEDICARE PATIENTS: Medicare does not cover prescription drugs with only a few exceptions. According to Medicare
regulations, | acknowledge that | am responsible for any drugs furnished to me while an outpatient that meet Medicare’s definition of a
prescription drug. These drugs are also referred to as self-administered but they may be administered to me by Hospital personnel.
Medicare requires Hospital to bill Medicare patients or other third party payers for these drugs. Medicare Part D beneficiaries may submit a
paper claim to the Medicare Part D Plan for possible reimbursement of these drugs in accordance with Medicare Drug Plan enrollment
materials.

treatment area may complicate or interfere with the course of treatment prescribed by my physician.

· G44.89 (ICD-10-CM) - Headache syndrome
PREVENTING SURGICAL SITE INFECTIONS
· Call 911 or have someone take you to the Emergency Department if you have any of the following: · Sudden numbness or weakness of your face, arm or leg especially on one side of the body
· Sudden confusion, difficulty speaking or trouble understanding
· Changes in your vision or loss of sight in one eye
· Sudden severe headache with no known cause
· Sudden dizziness, trouble walking, loss of balance or coordination

It is important to seek emergency care right away if you have further stroke symptoms. If you get
emergency help quickly, the powerful clot-dissolving medicines can reduce the disabilities caused by a
stroke. 
For more information:
American Stroke Association
1-888-4-STROKE
www.strokeassociation.org
IF YOU SMOKE OR USE TOBACCO PLEASE READ THE FOLLOWING:
Why is smoking bad for me?
Smoking increases the risk of heart disease, lung disease, vascular disease, stroke, and cancer. If you
smoke, STOP!
For more information:
Quit Now Kentucky
1-800-QUIT-NOW
https://kentucky.quitlogix.org/en-US/

Patient Instructions
None · Call 911 or have someone take you to the Emergency Department if you have any of the following: · Sudden numbness or weakness of your face, arm or leg especially on one side of the body
· Sudden confusion, difficulty speaking or trouble understanding
· Changes in your vision or loss of sight in one eye
· Sudden severe headache with no known cause
· Sudden dizziness, trouble walking, loss of balance or coordination
It is important to seek emergency care right away if you have further stroke symptoms. If you get
emergency help quickly, the powerful clot-dissolving medicines can reduce the disabilities caused by a
stroke. 
For more information:
American Stroke Association
1-888-4-STROKE
www.strokeassociation.org
IF YOU SMOKE OR USE TOBACCO PLEASE READ THE FOLLOWING:
Why is smoking bad for me?
Smoking increases the risk of heart disease, lung disease, vascular disease, stroke, and cancer. If you
smoke, STOP!
For more information:
Quit Now Kentucky
1-800-QUIT-NOW
https://kentucky.quitlogix.org/en-US/
If you feel like life is too tough and are thinking of suicide or injuring yourself, get help right away! · Call or text 988 to speak to someone.

EXAMINATION: CT CERVICAL SPINE WO CONTRAST- CLINICAL INDICATION: trauma

administration. Reformatted images in the coronal and/or sagittal
plane(s) were generated from the axial data set to facilitate diagnostic
accuracy and/or surgical planning. Radiation dose reduction techniques were utilized per ALARA protocol. Automated exposure control was initiated through either or CareDose or
DoseRight software packages by manufacturer protocol. 595.53 mGy.cm

Our mission is to provide you with high quality individualized care thatis safe and effective to meet your
healthcare needs. We will provide you with education and instruction to help with your pain
management/recovery, and we will strive for this to be a successfulprocess. This needs to be a team effort to
include not only your care providers at the hospital, but you, the patient.
Our care goals are:
e To work together in providing your treatment in a respectful manner.
e To help you meet the goals of your treatment plan.
e To maintain a safe environment for thedelivery of health care services at our hospital.
e To protect the safety and well-being of all patients, visitors, staff and care providers.
We value you as a patient and want to continue to provide you with high quality safe care and service. To do so,
we need to outline the expectations that will foster an effective provider- patient relationship.
The following expectations are required to maintain an effective provider- patient relationship.
Patient Behavior Expectations
e | will comply with the medical treatment plan and recommendations developed between my provider
and me.
e | will treat my care providers with dignity and respect, and | expect to be treated with dignity and respect.
e | understand that profanity, physical or verbal abuse, or any other form of violence against anyone will
not be allowed or tolerated.
e | understand that unauthorized and/or illegal drugs are not allowed in the hospital.
e For my safety, |will not leave the nursing unit where |am a patient during my treatment unless
accompanied by hospital personnel for diagnostic procedures or treatments.
e If leave the unit, itwill be considered going against medical advice and | maybe discharged from the
hospital. For any future medical care, |would need to consult my primary care MD/Provider or go to an
Emergency Room for further evaluation. Hospital staff have the right not to re-admit me for further care
unless an emergency situation exists.

INPATIENT AND OUTPATIENT CONDITIONS AND CONSENTS CSN: 91058394545 ADMIT DATE:042619
1. CONSENT TO DIAGNOSTIC TESTS, PROCEDURES, & MEDICALTREATMENT:| voluntarily consent to (and consent on behalf of any child born to
me while hospitalized) care involving routine diagnostic tests, procedures and medical treatment as ordered by my admitting and treating
physicians, including their assistants or designees. In the case of diagnostic studies, | consent to the interpretation of the studies at an off-site
location through the use of telehealth. | consent to medical, nursing, allied health and other students observing and participating in my care
under the supervision of a qualified professional. | consent to photographic recordings or reproducible images during the surgical, medical,
and/or diagnostic procedure(s) and their use for identification, scientific, educational, or research purposes. | also consent to testing for
communicable and blood-borne infectious diseases, such as hepatitis, tuberculosis, and the human immunodeficiency virus (HIV), if a provider
orders testing for diagnostic purposes or if there has been an exposure to healthcare personnel. | have been given no guarantees about the
results that may be obtained from my care.
2. PHYSICIANS AND OTHER HEALTH CARE PROVIDERS WHO ARE NOT EMPLOYEES OF THE HOSPITAL: Physicians and certified registered
nurse anesthetists rendering medical treatment to patients in the hospital are not employed by the Hospital. In most situations, advanced
practitioners, including but not limited to, nurse practitioners, physician assistants, surgical assistants and psychologists, providing
treatment to patients in the Hospital are not employed by the Hospital. You may receive a separate bill for the services of these providers, or
the Hospital bill you receive may have separate charges for services of such providers. Charges for such services are established by the
providers.
3. CONSENT TO USE AND DISCLOSURE OF INFORMATION: |authorize the Hospital and the health care providers on its medical staff to use
and disclose information about me to carry out treatment, payment and healthcare operations. Examples of such uses and disclosures
include, but are not limited to, providing information to: (a) any person, company, or entity (Such as HMOs, insurance companies, workers’
compensation carriers, my employer, the Medicare and Medicaid programs and their intermediaries and review organizations, and any
other payor or its review organization or third party administrator) that is or may be liable for paying any claim for benefits arising out of
services provided to me on an inpatient or outpatient basis, or for any ambulance services provided for my transport to or from the Hospital;
(b) any health care provider or ambulance company providing services for me; (c) any providers who may be providing follow-up care to me
after discharge such as home health agencies, nursing homes, physicians, and other health care facilities to which the patient may be
transferred; (d) any licensing or accrediting organizations necessary for the Hospital to obtain or maintain licensure or accreditation; (e) to
providers or entities for the purposes of conducting medical audits, utilization reviews, or quality assurance reviews; and (f) any other
persons or entities described in the Hospital’s Notice of Privacy Practices. This consent includes the release of medical records and billing
information related to drug-related conditions, alcoholism, psychological and psychiatric conditions, and/or communicable or blood-borne
infectious diseases, such as hepatitis, tuberculosis, and the human immunodeficiency virus (HIV). | also consent to any third party payor or
its review organization paying for my care to discuss my plan of treatment for utilization review purposes. | also authorize the release of my
social security number to the manufacturer of any implanted medical device in accordance with the Medical Device Tracking Act.
4. FINANCIAL AGREEMENT ANDASSIGNMENT OF BENEFITS:| agree that | am responsible for payment of my Hospital bill. Payment of any
portion of my bill not covered by a third party payor is due upon discharge from the Hospital unless the Hospital has agreed to other
arrangements. | agree to the assignment of all third party payor benefits to the Hospital and to any health care provider rendering services to
me. | agree to pay the Hospital and other health care providers for all charges for services that are not covered or paid by any third party
payor regardless of the reason, including but not limited to a determination by any third party payor that such services are not covered
services or medically necessary. To the extent | am a Medicare or Medicaid beneficiary, | certify that any information | provide in applying for
payment under Title XVIII (Medicare) or Title XIX (Medicaid) of the Social Security Act is correct. | request payment of authorized benefits to be
made on my behalf to Baptist Health facility or to any health care provider rendering services to me by the Medicare or Medicaid program. |
hereby irrevocably appoint Hospital as my authorized representative to pursue any claims, penalties, and administrative and/or legal
remedies on my behalf for collection against any responsible payer, employer-sponsored medical benefit plans, third party liability carrier or,
any other responsible third party for any and all benefits due me for the payment of charges associated with my treatment. This assignment
shall not be construed as an obligation of the Hospital to pursue any such right of recovery. | agree to take all actions necessary to assist
Hospital in collecting payment from any such third party payer. | understand that after reasonable notice, any unpaid account may be turned
over to a collection agency and/or attorney for collection. Should it be necessary for the Hospital to pursue collection, | agree to pay all
reasonable collection costs, including

5. OUTPATIENT MEDICARE PATIENTS: Medicare does not cover prescription drugs with only a few exceptions. According to Medicare
regulations, | acknowledge that | am responsible for any drugs furnished to me while an outpatient that meet Medicare’s definition of a
prescription drug. These drugs are also referred to as self-administered but they may be administered to me by Hospital personnel.
Medicare requires Hospital to bill Medicare patients or other third party payers for these drugs. Medicare Part D beneficiaries may submit a
paper claim to the Medicare Part D Plan for possible reimbursement of these drugs in accordance with Medicare Drug Plan enrollment
materials.

personnel, patients, and visitors on the Hospital’s campus. | understand that | am to remain in my treatment area at all times. | further
understand that if | leave my treatment area, | will not be under nursing or medical supervision, and further understand that leaving my
treatment area may complicate or interfere with the course of treatment prescribed by my physician.

(including gender identity and gender stereotyping), disability or any other status protected by federal, state or local law.

Muscle Strain Easy-to-Read (English)

wa BAPTIST HEALTH’ oe s

Baptist Health Corbin
1 Trillium Way

Mills, Amy
MRN: [PHONE-2460], DOB: [DOB-3254], Sex: F
Adm: 4/26/2019, D/C: 4/26/2019
[DOB-2461] - ED in Baptist Health Corbin Emergency Department (continued)
More Information
Muscle Strain
A muscle strain is an injury that happens when a muscle is stretched longer than normal. This can happen during a fall, 
sports, or lifting. This can tear some muscle fibers. Usually, recovery from muscle strain takes 1–2 weeks. Complete
healing normally takes 5–6 weeks. This condition is first treated with PRICE therapy. This involves: · Protecting your muscle from being injured again. · Resting your injured muscle. · Icing your injured muscle. · Applying pressure (compression) to your injured muscle. This may be done with a splint or elastic bandage. · Raising (elevating) your injured muscle.
Your doctor may also recommend medicine for pain.
Follow these instructions at home:
If you have a splint: · Wear the splint as told by your doctor. Take it off only as told by your doctor. · Loosen the splint if your fingers or toes tingle, get numb, or turn cold and blue. · Keep the splint clean. · If the splint is not waterproof:
○ Do not let it get wet.
○ Cover it with a watertight covering when you take a bath or a shower.
Managing pain, stiffness, and swelling
· If directed, put ice on your injured area.
○ If you have a removable splint, take it off as told by your doctor.
○ Put ice in a plastic bag.
○ Place a towel between your skin and the bag.
○ Leave the ice on for 20 minutes, 2–3 times a day. · Move your fingers or toes often. This helps to avoid stiffness and lessen swelling. · Raise your injured area above the level of your heart while you are sitting or lying down.

[DOB-2463] - ED in Baptist Health Corbin Emergency Department (continued)
More Information (continued)
·
· Wear an elastic bandage as told by your doctor. Make sure it is not too tight.
General instructions · Take over-the-counter and prescription medicines only as told by your doctor. · Limit your activity. Rest your injured muscle as told by your doctor. Your doctor may say that gentle 
movements are okay. · If physical therapy was prescribed, do exercises as told by your doctor. · Do not put pressure on any part of the splint until it is fully hardened. This may take many hours. · Do not use any products that contain nicotine or tobacco, such as cigarettes and e-cigarettes. These can delay 
bone healing. If you need help quitting, ask your doctor. · Warm up before you exercise. This helps to prevent more muscle strains. · Ask your doctor when it is safe to drive if you have a splint. · Keep all follow-up visits as told by your doctor. This is important.
Contact a doctor if: · You have more pain or swelling in your injured area.
Get help right away if: · You have any of these problems in your injured area:
○ You have numbness.
○ You have tingling.
○ You lose a lot of strength.
Summary
· A muscle strain is an injury that happens when a muscle is stretched longer than normal. · This condition is first treated with PRICE therapy. This includes protecting, resting, icing, adding pressure, and 
raising your injury. · Limit your activity. Rest your injured muscle as told by your doctor. Your doctor may say that gentle 
movements are okay. · Warm up before you exercise. This helps to prevent more muscle strains.

Elsevier Interactive Patient Education © 2019 Elsevier Inc. · Call 911 or have someone take you to the Emergency Department if you have any of the following: · Sudden numbness or weakness of your face, arm or leg especially on one side of the body
· Sudden confusion, difficulty speaking or trouble understanding
· Changes in your vision or loss of sight in one eye
· Sudden severe headache with no known cause
· Sudden dizziness, trouble walking, loss of balance or coordination
It is important to seek emergency care right away if you have further stroke symptoms. If you get
emergency help quickly, the powerful clot-dissolving medicines can reduce the disabilities caused by a
stroke. 
For more information:
American Stroke Association
1-888-4-STROKE
www.strokeassociation.org
IF YOU SMOKE OR USE TOBACCO PLEASE READ THE FOLLOWING:
Why is smoking bad for me?
Smoking increases the risk of heart disease, lung disease, vascular disease, stroke, and cancer. If you
smoke, STOP!
For more information:
Quit Now Kentucky
1-800-QUIT-NOW
https://kentucky.quitlogix.org/en-US/
If you feel like life is too tough and are thinking of suicide or injuring yourself, get help right away! · Call or text 988 to speak to someone.

03/30/19 1650 Registration Completed STORMS, MAKAYLA -- 03/30/19 1841 Patient discharged ROBERTS, AMANDA LEMARR -- 03/30/19 1841 ED Tracking End ROBERTS, AMANDA LEMARR -- ED Provider Note
Subjective
Patient is a 35-year-old female who presents the emergency department complaining of a severe headache located in
the back of her head for the past 2-3 days. The patient states that she went to urgent care earlier today and was told
that she needed to come to the emergency department for a CT scan due to her symptoms. She states that the pain
is located on the crown of her head, and is making her scalp very tender to touch. She states that she is unable to
even brush her hair due to this pain. She has never had these symptoms in the past and has no history of migraine
headaches. She denies any changes in vision, slurred speech, fever, chills or stiff neck. She has not had any
nausea, vomiting, shortness of air, dizziness or any additional symptoms. Review of Systems
Constitutional: Negative for activity change, appetite change, chills, diaphoresis, fatigue and fever. HENT: Negative for congestion, postnasal drip, rhinorrhea, sinus pressure, sinus pain, sneezing, sore throat and
tinnitus. 
Eyes: Negative for photophobia, pain, discharge, redness and itching. Respiratory: Negative for apnea, cough, choking, chest tightness, shortness of breath, wheezing and stridor. Cardiovascular: Negative for chest pain, palpitations and leg swelling. Gastrointestinal: Negative for abdominal distention, abdominal pain, constipation, diarrhea, nausea and vomiting. Endocrine: Negative for cold intolerance, heat intolerance and polydipsia. Genitourinary: Negative for difficulty urinating and dysuria. Musculoskeletal: Negative for arthralgias, back pain, myalgias, neck pain and neck stiffness. Skin: Negative for color change, pallor and rash. Allergic/Immunologic: Negative for environmental allergies, food allergies and immunocompromised state. Neurological: Positive for headaches. Negative for dizziness, seizures, syncope, facial asymmetry, speech difficulty, weakness, light-headedness and numbness.

Our
ee
mission is to provide
LE
you with high quality individualized care that
A
is safe and effective to meet your
healthcare needs. We will provide you with education and instruction to help with your pain
management/recovery, and we will strive for this to be a successfulprocess. This needs to be a team effort to
include not only your care providers at the hospital, but you, the patient.
Our care goals are:
e To work together in providing your treatment in a respectful manner.
e To help you meet the goals of your treatment plan.
e To maintain a safe environment for thedelivery of health care services at our hospital.
e To protect the safety and well-being of all patients, visitors, staff and care providers.
We value you as a patient and want to continue to provide you with high quality safe care and service. To do so,
we need to outline the expectations that will foster an effective provider- patient relationship.
The following expectations are required to maintain an effective provider- patient relationship.
Patient Behavior Expectations
e | will comply with the medical treatment plan and recommendations developed between my provider
and me.
e | will treat my care providers with dignity and respect, and | expect to be treated with dignity and respect.
e | understand that profanity, physical or verbal abuse, or any other form of violence against anyone will
not be allowed or tolerated.
e | understand that unauthorized and/or illegal drugs are not allowed in the hospital.
e For my safety, |will not leave the nursing unit where |am a patient during my treatment unless
accompanied by hospital personnel for diagnostic procedures or treatments.
e If leave the unit, itwill be considered going against medical advice and | maybe discharged from the
hospital. For any future medical care, |would need to consult my primary care MD/Provider or go to an
Emergency Room for further evaluation. Hospital staff have the right not to re-admit me for further care
unless an emergency situation exists.

INPATIENT AND OUTPATIENT CONDITIONS AND CONSENTS Saadsam1 Oni wu(TRAMQUINT NADalta
1. CONSENT TO DIAGNOSTIC TESTS, PROCEDURES, & MEDICALTREATMENT:| voluntarily consent to (and consent on behalf of any child born to
me while hospitalized) care involving routine diagnostic tests, procedures and medical treatment as ordered by my admitting and treating
physicians, including their assistants or designees. In the case of diagnostic studies, | consent to the interpretation of the studies at an off-site
location through the use of telehealth. | consent to medical, nursing, allied health and other students observing and participating in my care
under the supervision of a qualified professional. | consent to photographic recordings or reproducible images during the surgical, medical,
and/or diagnostic procedure(s) and their use for identification, scientific, educational, or research purposes. | also consent to testing for
communicable and blood-borne infectious diseases, such as hepatitis, tuberculosis, and the human immunodeficiency virus (HIV), if a provider
orders testing for diagnostic purposes or if there has been an exposure to healthcare personnel. | have been given no guarantees about the
results that may be obtained from my care.
2. PHYSICIANS AND OTHER HEALTH CARE PROVIDERS WHO ARE NOT EMPLOYEES OF THE HOSPITAL: Physicians and certified registered
nurse anesthetists rendering medical treatment to patients in the hospital are not employed by the Hospital. In most situations, advanced
practitioners, including but not limited to, nurse practitioners, physician assistants, surgical assistants and psychologists, providing
treatment to patients in the Hospital are not employed by the Hospital. You may receive a separate bill for the services of these providers, or
the Hospital bill you receive may have separate charges for services of such providers. Charges for such services are established by the
providers.
3. CONSENT TO USE AND DISCLOSURE OF INFORMATION: |authorize the Hospital and the health care providers on its medical staff to use
and disclose information about me to carry out treatment, payment and healthcare operations. Examples of such uses and disclosures
include, but are not limited to, providing information to: (a) any person, company, or entity (Such as HMOs, insurance companies, workers’
compensation carriers, my employer, the Medicare and Medicaid programs and their intermediaries and review organizations, and any
other payor or its review organization or third party administrator) that is or may be liable for paying any claim for benefits arising out of
services provided to me on an inpatient or outpatient basis, or for any ambulance services provided for my transport to or from the Hospital;
(b) any health care provider or ambulance company providing services for me; (c) any providers who may be providing follow-up care to me
after discharge such as home health agencies, nursing homes, physicians, and other health care facilities to which the patient may be
transferred; (d) any licensing or accrediting organizations necessary for the Hospital to obtain or maintain licensure or accreditation; (e) to
providers or entities for the purposes of conducting medical audits, utilization reviews, or quality assurance reviews; and (f) any other
persons or entities described in the Hospital’s Notice of Privacy Practices. This consent includes the release of medical records and billing
information related to drug-related conditions, alcoholism, psychological and psychiatric conditions, and/or communicable or blood-borne
infectious diseases, such as hepatitis, tuberculosis, and the human immunodeficiency virus (HIV). | also consent to any third party payor or
its review organization paying for my care to discuss my plan of treatment for utilization review purposes. | also authorize the release of my
social security number to the manufacturer of any implanted medical device in accordance with the Medical Device Tracking Act.
4. FINANCIAL AGREEMENT ANDASSIGNMENT OF BENEFITS:| agree that | am responsible for payment of my Hospital bill. Payment of any
portion of my bill not covered by a third party payor is due upon discharge from the Hospital unless the Hospital has agreed to other
arrangements. | agree to the assignment of all third party payor benefits to the Hospital and to any health care provider rendering services to
me. | agree to pay the Hospital and other health care providers for all charges for services that are not covered or paid by any third party
payor regardless of the reason, including but not limited to a determination by any third party payor that such services are not covered
services or medically necessary. To the extent | am a Medicare or Medicaid beneficiary, | certify that any information | provide in applying for
payment under Title XVIII (Medicare) or Title XIX (Medicaid) of the Social Security Act is correct. | request payment of authorized benefits to be
made on my behalf to Baptist Health facility or to any health care provider rendering services to me by the Medicare or Medicaid program. |
hereby irrevocably appoint Hospital as my authorized representative to pursue any claims, penalties, and administrative and/or legal
remedies on my behalf for collection against any responsible payer, employer-sponsored medical benefit plans, third party liability carrier or,
any other responsible third party for any and all benefits due me for the payment of charges associated with my treatment. This assignment

[DOB-2602] - ED in Baptist Health Corbin Emergency Department (continued)
More Information
Occipital Neuralgia
Occipital neuralgia is a type of headache that causes episodes of very bad pain in the back of your head. Pain from 
occipital neuralgia may spread (radiate) to other parts of your head. The pain is usually brief and often goes away after 
you rest and relax.
These headaches may be caused by irritation of the nerves that leave your spinal cord high up in your neck, just below 
the base of your skull (occipital nerves). Your occipital nerves transmit sensations from the back of your head, the top 
of your head, and the areas behind your ears.
What are the causes?
Occipital neuralgia can occur without any known cause (primary headache syndrome). In other cases, occipital 
neuralgia is caused by pressure on or irritation of one of the two occipital nerves. Causes of occipital nerve 
compression or irritation include: · Wear and tear of the vertebrae in the neck (osteoarthritis). · Neck injury. · Disease of the disks that separate the vertebrae. · Tumors. · Gout. · Infections. · Diabetes. · Swollen blood vessels that put pressure on the occipital nerves. · Muscle spasm in the neck.
What are the signs or symptoms?
Pain is the main symptom of occipital neuralgia. It usually starts in the back of the head but may also be felt in other 
areas supplied by the occipital nerves. Pain is usually on one side but may be on both sides. You may have: · Brief episodes of very bad pain that is burning, stabbing, shocking, or shooting. · Pain behind the eye. · Pain triggered by neck movement or hair brushing. · Scalp tenderness. · Aching in the back of the head between episodes of very bad pain.
How is this diagnosed?
Your health care provider may diagnose occipital neuralgia based on your symptoms and a physical exam. During the 
exam, the health care provider may push on areas supplied by the occipital nerves to see if they are painful. Some tests 
may also be done to help in making the diagnosis. These may include: · Imaging studies of the upper spinal cord, such as an MRI or CT scan. These may show compression or spinal 
Printed on 1/19/23 12:43 PM Page 549
wa BAPTIST HEALTH’ oe s

Baptist Health Corbin
1 Trillium Way
Corbin KY 40701-8727
